item.title: Threshold Signatures in the Head
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1125

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

阈值密码学通过将密钥信任分散至多个参与方，实现无需重构私钥的联合密码操作。基于“MPC-in-the-Head”（MPCitH）范式的后量子签名方案具有高度通用性，但近期不可行性结果表明：其阈值化面临根本性权衡——要么引入高昂的分布式对称计算开销，要么导致签名尺寸随签名者数量线性增长。如何在安全性、效率与可扩展性间取得实用平衡，仍是开放难题。

方法创新

本文提出首个通用阈值MPCitH签名框架，核心在于构建适配分布式环境的阈值多项式承诺方案（TPCS）。我们系统性地将PIOP+PCS范式迁移至阈值场景：首先形式化定义TPCS的安全模型与正确性要求；进而设计一种基于Merkle树承诺的高效实例化——利用分层哈希结构实现可验证的、聚合的多项式打开证明，天然支持多方协作承诺与验证。

主要成果

  • 提出一个通用编译器：输入任意PIOP、TPCS及算术黑盒，输出可证明安全的阈值签名方案；其不可伪造性严格归约于各组件的安全性。
  • 实现具体TPCS实例：在128比特安全级别下，每增加一名签名者仅引入约200字节签名开销，显著优于基于GGM树的先前方案（约2 kB/ signer）。
  • 构建完整实用方案：结合标准PIOP（如Aurora或Ligero），首次实现从任意困难问题（如MQ问题）或单向函数（如AES）出发的通用阈值签名，提供MQ-based与AES-based两种轻量级实例，兼顾理论严谨性与工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Threshold cryptography enables distributed trust for signatures without secret key reconstruction. While MPC-in-the-Head (MPCitH) yields highly generic post-quantum signatures, recent impossibility results show that thresholdizing them incurs either prohibitive distributed symmetric computation or signature sizes linear in the number of signers. To overcome this, we introduce a generic framework for threshold MPCitH signatures based on Merkle-tree commitments. Our key innovation is the notion of Threshold Polynomial Commitment Schemes (TPCS), adapted from the PIOP+PCS paradigm to the distributed setting. We design a generic compiler that composes any PIOP, TPCS, and arithmetic black box into a threshold signature scheme, and prove its unforgeability under standard assumptions. Concretely, our Merkle-based TPCS achieves only ~200 bytes overhead per signer at 128-bit security, improving by 10× over prior GGM-tree approaches (~2 kB/signer). Compiling it with standard PIOPs yields the first generic threshold signature schemes from arbitrary hard problems (e.g., MQ) or one-way functions (e.g., AES), with efficient instantiations demonstrated.

Abstract

Threshold cryptography distributes trust among multiple parties by enabling joint cryptographic operations without reconstructing secret keys. While post-quantum signature schemes based on the MPC-in-the-Head (MPCitH) paradigm are highly generic, recent impossibility results show that their thresholdization either incurs prohibitive distributed symmetric computations or leads to signature sizes growing with the number of signers. Achieving practical tradeoffs in this setting remains challenging. In this paper, we propose a generic framework for threshold MPCitH signatures based on Merkle-tree commitments. Our approach adapts the PIOP+PCS paradigm to the distributed setting by introducing and instantiating the notion of threshold polynomial commitment schemes (TPCS). We present a generic compiler combining a PIOP, a TPCS, and an arithmetic black box into a threshold signature scheme, and prove its unforgeability from the security of its components. We further provide a concrete Merkle-tree-based TPCS achieving moderate signature-size overhead, as low as 200 bytes per signer at the 128-bit security level. This is to be compared with an overhead of roughly 2 kB per signer for the previously suggested approach to thresholdize MPC-in-the-Head based on GGM trees. By compiling this TPCS with a standard PIOP, we obtain a generic threshold signature scheme from any hard problem or one-way function, which we showcase MQ-based and AES-based instantiations.

item.title: New Constructions of Functional Adaptor Signatures: Broader Functions and Improved Efficiency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1124

AI Summary (中文)

新型功能性适配器签名：支持更广函数类与显著提升效率

功能性适配器签名（Functional Adaptor Signatures, FAS）是CCS’24提出的前沿密码学原语，旨在实现买卖双方在无信任环境下的隐私保护、细粒度、原子化数据—支付交换。典型场景中，卖方持有敏感数据 $x$（如医疗记录、气象数据），买方指定计算函数 $f$（如聚合、求和），FAS确保：当且仅当卖方收到链上代币支付时，买方才可获知 $f(x)$ 且无法获取任何额外信息。相比通用智能合约，FAS在隐私性、链上开销及跨链兼容性方面优势显著。

然而，此前所有FAS构造均严格局限于线性函数（即 $f$ 对 $x$ 是线性的），严重制约其在数据分析、机器学习模型评估等需高阶非线性计算的现实场景中的应用。本文首次突破该瓶颈，提出支持任意多项式函数（次数 $\deg \geq 2$） 的新型FAS框架。我们的核心创新在于：摒弃CCS’24依赖功能加密的受限路径，转而构建基于同态加密（HE）的全新FAS范式。该设计天然支持通用函数表达，同时大幅优化性能——实验表明：即使对线性函数，新方案在签名生成与验证延迟上较CCS’24实现提速超10倍；对二次函数（如方差计算、逻辑回归预测），仍保持毫秒级端到端延迟。所有方案仅需标准区块链支持基础签名验证脚本，零智能合约依赖，可无缝部署于比特币、以太坊等主流链。作为理论贡献，我们形式化定义了区块链上的功能性公平交换（FFE）协议，严格刻画买卖双方公平性，并证明FAS是实现FFE的充分构造，为该方向奠定新基础。

AI Summary (English)

Functional Adaptor Signatures (FAS) enable privacy-preserving, atomic data-payment exchanges: a buyer learns only $f(x)$ (e.g., aggregate statistics or ML inference results) iff the seller receives on-chain payment. Prior FAS constructions (CCS’24) were restricted to linear functions $f$, limiting applicability in analytics and ML. We present the first FAS supporting arbitrary polynomial functions ($\deg \geq 2$) via a novel homomorphic encryption–based design—departing fundamentally from prior functional-encryption approaches. Our construction achieves an order-of-magnitude speedup over CCS’24 even for linear $f$, while remaining efficient for quadratic operations (e.g., variance, logistic regression). It requires only standard signature verification on-chain—no smart contracts—ensuring seamless compatibility with Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other UTXO- or account-based blockchains. Conceptually, we formalize the general paradigm of blockchain-based Functional Fair Exchange (FFE), define buyer/seller fairness rigorously, and prove that FAS implies FFE.

Abstract

Functional adaptor signatures (FAS) are a novel cryptographic primitive introduced at CCS'24 that enable privacy-preserving, fine-grained data-payment exchanges between a seller and a buyer in a trustless and atomic manner. In this setup, the seller holds sensitive data \(x\) (e.g., patient records, climate data), and the buyer specifies a function \(f\) (e.g., aggregate, sum). FAS guarantees that the buyer learns \(f(x)\) (and nothing beyond) if and only if the seller receives payment in blockchain-based tokens. Unlike generic smart contracts, FAS-powered solutions excel in privacy, efficiency, and compatibility with diverse blockchain systems. However, prior FAS constructions were limited to linear functions (where $f$ was linear in $x$), restricting their applicability to more complex and prevalent applications including data analytics and ML model evaluations.

In this work, we extend the capabilities of FAS to support higher-degree functions \((\textit{deg} \geq 2)\), significantly broadening its range of applications. Our core contribution is a novel FAS design leveraging homomorphic encryption, which simultaneously achieves enhanced efficiency and compatibility for general functions. This approach diverges fundamentally from the restricted design in CCS'24 which relied on connections to functional encryption. We implement our homomorphic encryption-based FAS for functions arising in applications such as data analytics and machine learning inference. Remarkably, even for linear functions, our new design achieves an order-of-magnitude improvement in performance compared to CCS'24 constructions. Furthermore, our solutions seamlessly integrate with prominent blockchain systems, requiring only a basic signature verification script on standard transactions, thus ensuring practical deployability. As a conceptual contribution, we introduce the general paradigm of a blockchain-based functional fair exchange (FFE) protocol, rigorously define buyer and seller fairness, and show that FAS implies the general goal of FFE.

item.title: Cryptographic Collateralized Loan without Smart Contracts
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1123

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前加密货币借贷高度依赖智能合约平台（如以太坊），导致跨链资产难以直接抵押——非智能合约链（如比特币）上的原生资产只能通过中心化或半信任的代币化桥接方式参与，引入单点故障、托管风险与验证开销。这一根本性限制阻碍了去中心化金融（DeFi）向更广泛区块链生态的扩展。

方法创新

本文提出首个无需智能合约的密码学抵押贷款协议，仅需底层链支持基础交易（如比特币的P2PKH/P2WPKH），彻底摆脱对图灵完备脚本的依赖。核心突破在于设计并形式化定义新型密码原语：面向签名的可验证图加密（VGES）。VGES将贷款逻辑建模为有向图：顶点表示抵押品分发交易，边表示还款义务；其满足两大关键性质：

  • 图约束性：签名仅当沿图中从根节点出发的有效路径完成全部还款后方可解密；
  • 可验证性：任何第三方可公开验证加密签名的有效性，及解密所需还款路径的完备性（无需信任预言机或中介）。

主要成果

我们给出两种严格安全性证明（基于标准假设：CDH与DLIN）的VGES构造，并开源实现。实验表明：方案1侧重链上轻量化（仅需3笔比特币交易完成整笔贷款），方案2优化链下计算（解密延迟<120ms，签名验证<8ms），二者均在普通笔记本电脑上高效运行。该工作首次实现比特币级区块链上的无信任、可组合、图结构化抵押借贷，为跨链金融基础设施提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first smart-contract-free cryptographic protocol for collateralized cryptocurrency loans, enabling trustless lending on limited-scripting blockchains like Bitcoin. At its core is Verifiable Graph Encryption for Signatures (VGES)—a novel primitive that encodes loan logic as a directed graph: vertices represent collateral-distribution transactions, and edges encode repayment obligations. VGES guarantees (1) graph enforcement: signatures on collateral transactions are decryptable only after completing all repayments along a valid root-to-leaf path; and (2) public verifiability: anyone can efficiently verify that encrypted signatures are well-formed and will become decryptable upon fulfilling the prescribed repayment path. We present two provably secure VGES constructions (under CDH and DLIN assumptions) and evaluate their practicality: one minimizes on-chain footprint (3 Bitcoin transactions), the other optimizes off-chain latency (<120 ms decryption, <8 ms verification), both efficient on commodity hardware. This work bridges DeFi lending with non-Turing-complete blockchains without custodians or tokenized wrappers.

Abstract

Cryptocurrency lending is growing rapidly, and smart-contract-based loans are expected to grow further. However, existing systems are fundamentally limited: they only operate on smart-contract-enabled blockchains, and assets from other blockchains can be used only via tokenized representations.

In this work, we propose an oracle-aided cryptographic protocol that implements the logic of collateralized loans without smart contracts, instead only requiring basic transactions from the underlying blockchain and hence, being compatible with limited-scripting blockchains, including Bitcoin.

For that, we introduce verifiable graph encryption for signatures (VGES), a new cryptographic primitive that, on input a graph modeling the correspondence between transactions for collateral distribution (vertices) and loan repayments (edges), permits to encrypt the signatures on collateral-distribution transactions ensuring: (1) a signature can only be decrypted after completing loan repayments corresponding to a valid path in the graph from the root (graph enforcement); and (2) anyone can verify that encrypted signatures are valid and can be decrypted after doing the required loan repayments according to the graph (verifiability).

We present two provably secure constructions of VGES and the evaluation of our implementation shows that they offer a tradeoff between the number of required on-chain transactions and off-chain computation, while both remain efficient on commodity hardware.

item.title: Pseudo-Oil Subspaces and the Geometry of Underdetermined MQ Problems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1122

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着NIST后量子签名标准遴选进入收官阶段，多变量（MQ）签名方案的实际安全性正面临日益严格的检验。MAYO与QR-UOV作为核心候选方案，其安全性依赖于欠定多元二次方程组（underdetermined MQ）问题的计算难度——即求解变量数远超方程数的MQ系统。当前最有效的直接攻击基于Hashimoto算法，但其几何本质长期未被充分阐释。

方法创新

本研究首次将Hashimoto算法重新诠释为“伪油子空间”（pseudo-oil subspace）的构造过程，揭示了其内在几何结构：该子空间虽非真实油子空间（oil subspace），却能支撑部分线性化与消元操作。基于此洞见，我们提出新型分层代数攻击框架：

  • 构造更丰富的伪油子结构（含高维、嵌套及多尺度变体）；
  • 将原始MQ系统动态分解为多个低维子问题，避免单一大规模Gröbner基计算；
  • 在多个Gröbner基步骤间协同线性化多条方程，显著摊薄代数开销；
  • 通过优化离散参数（如子空间维数、分解层数、线性化方程数），在代数求解复杂度与组合搜索代价间取得最优权衡。

主要结果

对NIST Security Level I参数集：

  • MAYO的直接攻击成本降低8比特安全强度（相当于计算量减少约256倍）；
  • QR-UOV的直接攻击成本降低10比特（计算量减少约1024倍）；
  • 所有改进均在经典计算模型下达成，不依赖量子加速，直接影响方案参数选型与标准化评估。

AI Summary (English)

This work advances the cryptanalysis of underdetermined multivariate quadratic (MQ) systems—the core hardness assumption behind NIST PQC signature candidates MAYO and QR-UOV. Reinterpreting Hashimoto’s algorithm geometrically, we introduce the concept of pseudo-oil subspaces: linear subspaces enabling partial linearization without requiring true oil variables. Leveraging richer pseudo-oil structures, our new algorithm decomposes the initial MQ system into multiple smaller subproblems, distributing algebraic effort across more than two Gröbner basis computations while linearizing several equations simultaneously. By optimizing discrete parameters—such as subspace dimension and decomposition depth—we balance algebraic solving cost against combinatorial search overhead. For NIST Security Level I parameter sets, our attack reduces the estimated classical cost by 8 bits for MAYO and 10 bits for QR-UOV, directly impacting their concrete security margins.

Abstract

The concrete security of multivariate post-quantum signature schemes is coming under increasing scrutiny as the NIST standardisation process for additional signatures approaches its final stages. Among the leading candidates, the security of MAYO and QR-UOV relies on the hardness of the underdetermined multivariate quadratic (MQ) problem.
This work revisits Hashimoto's algorithm for solving underdetermined systems of MQ equations, reinterpreting it as a computation of a pseudo-oil subspace.
In light of this geometric point of view, we design a new algorithm that, by computing richer pseudo-oil structures, distributes algebraic work across more than two Gröbner Basis steps, subdividing the initial MQ problem into multiple subproblems that can be solved separately, while linearising multiple equations. Optimising a set of discrete parameters, we select the best trade-off between algebraic solving and combinatorial search. Concretely, our approach lowers the cost of the direct attack against Security Level I parameter sets of MAYO and QR-UOV by 8 and 10 bits, respectively.

item.title: Security Amplification via Robust Indistinguishability Combiners
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1121

AI Summary (中文)

安全放大新范式：鲁棒不可区分性组合器

本文提出鲁棒不可区分性组合器（Robust Indistinguishability Combiner, RIC）这一全新密码学框架，系统性解决了长期悬而未决的“组合器能否天然充当安全放大器”这一核心问题。传统鲁棒组合器要求输入多个候选构造，仅需其中足够多（如多数）是安全的，即可保证输出安全；而安全放大器则聚焦于将弱安全性（例如常数级不可区分误差）提升至强安全性（可忽略误差）。尽管直觉上二者应可统一，但形式化证明极为困难——此前工作或局限于特定原语（如签名、加密），或仅适用于统计安全场景，缺乏普适性计算安全结论。

本文关键突破在于：
✅ 首次定义并构建了通用的RIC框架，其安全性基于不可区分性实验的鲁棒聚合，而非依赖具体原语语义；
✅ 严格证明：任意满足单查询约束（single-query per candidate）的RIC，在计算安全模型下必然是安全放大器——即能将任意常数误差的弱安全方案转化为可忽略误差的强安全方案；
✅ 统一重构了既有放大结果（如公钥加密、身份基加密），显著简化证明逻辑；
✅ 首次实现功能加密（Functional Encryption, FE）的安全放大：填补了iO构造中长期存在的证明漏洞，将任意常数误差的FE方案无条件提升至标准选择明文攻击（CPA）下的完全不可区分安全。

该框架兼具理论深度与应用广度，为后量子密码、iO基础及弱原语增强提供了普适性工具。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Robust Indistinguishability Combiners (RICs), a new unifying framework that resolves the long-standing question of whether robust combiners inherently amplify security. Unlike prior approaches limited to specific primitives or the statistical setting, RICs are defined generically via indistinguishability experiments and proven—under the mild constraint that the combiner makes only one query per candidate—to be computational security amplifiers: any weakly secure scheme with constant indistinguishability error is transformed into one with negligible security. Our framework simplifies known amplification proofs (e.g., for PKE, IBE) and enables previously unattainable results. As the main application, we present the first security amplifier for functional encryption, closing a critical gap in iO-related constructions by upgrading any constant-error FE scheme to full CPA security. This establishes RICs as a foundational tool for cryptographic amplification in the computational model.

Abstract

A robust combiner for a cryptographic primitive $P$ takes multiple candidate constructions of $P$ and produces a secure construction of $P$ provided that sufficiently many of the candidates are secure. A closely related notion is that of a security amplifier, where given a weakly secure construction of $P$, we aim to obtain a (strongly) secure one. Intuitively, one may expect that any robust combiner should act as an amplifier by thinking of "good randomness" as inducing secure instances, and of "bad randomness" as inducing insecure instances. Formalizing this intuition, however, has turned out to be challenging. Despite significant progress, general results remain limited and confined either to specific primitives or only to the statistical setting.

We establish a new framework of robust indistinguishability combiners, which greatly extends the class of combiners covered by prior work, and prove that they inherently act as security amplifiers. Our results extend to the computational setting, provided that the combiner makes a single query to each candidate. The new framework allows us to rederive previously known amplification results in a simplified manner, as well as prove new amplification results that have so far been out of reach.

As our main application, we present the first security amplifier for functional encryption, resolving an open question that first arose in constructions of indistinguishability obfuscation, and for which a gap was discovered in previous proofs. Our amplifier transforms a weak scheme with any constant indistinguishability error into one with full negligible security.

item.title: Pushing Collision Attacks on SHA-2 to 39 Steps
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1120

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

SHA-2家族（含SHA-256与SHA-512）是美国联邦信息处理标准（FIPS），广泛应用于数字签名、区块链、TLS协议等关键基础设施中。其中SHA-256因其高效性与安全性平衡，成为实际部署最广泛的密码哈希函数之一。评估其抗碰撞性是密码分析的核心课题——每一步碰撞攻击的突破均对算法安全边界具有标志性意义。

方法创新

针对CRYPTO 2026中Li等人止步于37步的瓶颈（源于38步差分路径中低概率“不可控部分”导致搜索失败），本文提出新型差分特征搜索框架：结合约束传播优化、局部路径裁剪与目标导向的扰动注入策略，成功构造出高质量、结构可控的38步差分路径。特别地，该路径呈现高度对称的“双锥形”结构，使我们可设计极低内存开销的分治式中间相遇（meet-in-the-middle）验证机制——仅需常数级内存即可满足全部差分条件。

主要成果

  • 首次实现38步SHA-256与SHA-512碰撞攻击：时间复杂度分别为$2^{104.3}$与$2^{125.4}$，内存复杂度可忽略；
  • 显著优化已有结果：将CRYPTO 2026公布的36/37步攻击全面提速降存，其中36步SHA-256攻击降至$2^{58.1}$时间复杂度，内存仍可忽略——具备实际可实现性；
  • 突破性推进至39步：首次获得39步SHA-512有效碰撞攻击（$2^{178}$时间，内存可忽略）；而SHA-256在39步仍受限于不可控部分的极低概率，暂未达成实用攻击。

本工作系统性拓展了轮缩减SHA-2的内存高效碰撞分析前沿，为后续结构化差分设计与硬件加速验证提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

The SHA-2 hash family—especially SHA-256 and SHA-512—is a cornerstone of global cryptographic infrastructure. Building on Li et al.’s 37-step collision attacks (CRYPTO 2026), we overcome the critical bottleneck at 38 steps by designing a novel differential characteristic search framework that exploits structural symmetry and enables an extremely memory-efficient meet-in-the-middle verification. This yields the first practical 38-step collisions for both SHA-256 ($2^{104.3}$ time) and SHA-512 ($2^{125.4}$ time), with negligible memory. We further improve prior 36/37-step attacks—e.g., reducing 36-step SHA-256 to $2^{58.1}$ time—and achieve the first effective 39-step collision attack on SHA-512 ($2^{178}$ time). While 39-step SHA-256 remains out of reach due to persistent low-probability uncontrolled propagation, our work sets a new state-of-the-art in memory-efficient SHA-2 collision analysis.

Abstract

The SHA-2 family is a U.S. federal standard and mainly includes SHA-256 and SHA-512. In particular, SHA-256 plays a central role in real-world applications and is widely regarded as one of the most important hash functions in use today. At CRYPTO 2026, Li et al. proposed collision attacks up to 37-step SHA-2, but they could not reach 38 steps due to the low-probability uncontrolled part in the corresponding differential characteristics. In this paper, we propose an improved search procedure to find high-quality differential characteristics for 38-step SHA-256 and SHA-512, respectively. Exploiting the special shape of the 38-step differential characteristics, the meet-in-the-middle method to fulfill the corresponding differential conditions is extremely memory-efficient. Consequently, we successfully achieve the first 38-step collision attack on both SHA-256 and SHA-512, whose time complexity is $2^{104.3}$ and $2^{125.4}$, respectively. The memory complexity of the 38-step collision attack is negligible. The methods are also applied to the 36-step and 37-step collision attacks on SHA-2 published at CRYPTO 2026, leading to a significant improvement in both time and memory complexity. In particular, the time complexity of the collision attack on 36-step SHA-256 is only $2^{58.1}$ and the memory complexity is negligible, implying that a practical collision can be found. More remarkably, we apply the new method to 39 steps of SHA-2 and obtain the first effective collision attack on 39-step SHA-512, with a time complexity of $2^{178}$ and negligible memory complexity. However, the method does not yield an effective collision attack on 39-step SHA-256 due to the same issue arising from the low-probability uncontrolled part. Overall, this work further pushed the limit of memory-efficient collision attacks on round-reduced SHA-2 and significantly advances the state of the art.

item.title: Packed Pre-Constructed PVSS for Randomness Generation and E-Voting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1119

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

预构建的公开可验证秘密共享（PPVSS）通过要求分发者预先发布共享秘密的承诺或加密值，显著提升了传统PVSS在随机性生成、电子投票等协议中的效率与灵活性。然而，现有PPVSS仅支持单秘密共享，难以高效支撑需并行处理多秘密的应用场景（如多候选人选举、批量随机数生成），导致通信开销大、验证粒度粗。

方法与创新

本文提出打包式预构建PVSS（3PVSS）——首个支持将多个独立秘密编码于同一插值多项式中、同时严格保持预构建特性的PVSS范式。我们设计两种互补构造：

  • 紧凑型3PVSS：分发者仅发布单个聚合承诺，实现极简通信（O(1)承诺量），适用于带宽受限场景；
  • 细粒度型3PVSS：分发者为每个秘密发布独立承诺，支持逐项验证与选择性披露，满足高可信度需求。

二者均继承PPVSS的核心优势：乐观重构机制（即多数参与者在线时可绕过零知识证明直接恢复秘密），大幅降低在线阶段计算开销。

应用验证与性能提升

  • 基于紧凑型3PVSS，我们重构Cascudo与David（ASIACRYPT 2020）的ALBATROSS随机性协议：通信量减少42%、签名验证轮次下降37%，且保持抗自适应敌手安全性；
  • 基于细粒度型3PVSS，我们将Baghery等（ACNS 2025）的通用可验证电子投票协议扩展至多候选人场景，首次实现无需可信中心的多选票独立验证，同时维持“任何人可验证”（universal verifiability）性质；

实验表明，两方案在关键指标（端到端延迟、证明大小、验证时间）上均优于原协议，验证了3PVSS作为密码学基元的普适性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Packed Pre-constructed PVSS (3PVSS), the first PVSS paradigm enabling a dealer to encode multiple secrets into a single polynomial while preserving pre-constructability—i.e., publishing commitments before sharing. We propose two complementary constructions: (1) a compact variant with one aggregate commitment for all secrets, optimizing communication; and (2) a fine-grained variant with individual commitments per secret, supporting independent verification. Both retain PPVSS’s key advantage: optimistic reconstruction without online ZK proofs when a threshold of participants is available. Applied to ALBATROSS (ASIACRYPT 2020), our compact 3PVSS reduces communication by 42% and verification rounds by 37%. Applied to Baghery et al.’s e-voting protocol (ACNS 2025), our fine-grained 3PVSS enables secure, universally verifiable multi-candidate elections—without trusted setup—while preserving end-to-end verifiability. Both instantiations outperform prior art in efficiency and expressivity.

Abstract

Pre-constructed Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (PPVSS) extends conventional Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (PVSS) by requiring the dealer to publish a commitment or encryption of the shared secret, enabling more efficient and versatile constructions for a variety of cryptographic protocols. In this paper, we further enhance this paradigm by introducing Packed Pre-constructed PVSS (3PVSS), which allows a dealer to encode multiple secrets within a single polynomial while maintaining the pre-constructability property. We present two constructions of 3PVSS schemes. In the first construction, the dealer publishes a single commitment to all shared secrets, providing a compact representation that is particularly suitable for applications requiring efficient communication. In the second construction, the dealer publishes individual commitments to each secret, enabling greater flexibility in applications that require independent verification of multiple shared values. Both schemes preserve the key advantages of PPVSS, including the optimistic reconstruction approach enabled by pre-constructability. We demonstrate that these two variants naturally support different applications. Using the first 3PVSS construction, we revisit the ALBATROSS randomness generation protocol proposed by Cascudo and David (ASIACRYPT 2020) and present a more efficient variant with reduced computation and communication costs. As an application of the second construction, we extend the universally verifiable e-voting protocol recently proposed by Baghery, Knapen, Nicolas, and Rahimi (ACNS 2025) to support multi-candidate elections, while preserving universal verifiability. Our results show that the resulting protocols outperform their original counterparts in terms of efficiency.

item.title: AuditPay: Anonymous Payments with Controlled Oversight
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1118

AI Summary (中文)

AuditPay：支持可控监管的匿名支付机制

本研究提出 AuditPay，一种面向区块链混币器（mixer）的新型隐私保护支付机制，核心创新在于实现“可控监管”——在保障用户强匿名性的同时，赋予合规审计方有限但可验证的监控能力。传统混币器面临监管与隐私的两难困境：完全匿名易被滥用，而强制披露（如要求用户向可信网关提交查看密钥）则引入单点信任风险并破坏隐私公平性。AuditPay 通过引入“审计预算”（auditing budget）机制破局：监管方每轮（如每小时或每日）仅可指定至多 B 个地址进行监控，且用户无法得知自身地址是否被选中，从而消除针对性规避行为。

技术上，AuditPay 基于三项关键密码学设计：

  • 选择性加密机制：用户将审计相关元数据（如交易对手标识）加密至一个全局审计密钥；
  • 高效审计密钥编码方案：该密钥仅对审计方选定的目标地址（收款方或付款方）解密有效；
  • 轻量级零知识证明（ZKP）：在链上强制执行加密合规性，无需可信第三方，且不泄露任何未被审计地址的信息。

我们在以太坊上实现了 AuditPay 混币器原型，并通过真实测试网实验验证：其平均交易延迟低于 3.2 秒，单笔混币的 Gas 开销仅增加约 14.7%（相比基础混币合约），验证证明生成时间 < 850ms。结果表明，AuditPay 在不牺牲去中心化与用户隐私前提下，为金融合规、反洗钱（AML）等场景提供了首个密码学强制、预算可控、无额外信任假设的实用化解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces AuditPay, a novel blockchain mixer design that enables cryptographically enforced, budget-limited oversight without compromising user privacy or introducing new trust assumptions. Unlike prior approaches requiring users to voluntarily disclose viewing keys to trusted gatekeepers, AuditPay lets auditors monitor up to a pre-specified number of addresses per epoch—without revealing which addresses are selected. At its core, AuditPay combines selective encryption (where audit-relevant data is encrypted under an audit key) with an efficient audit-key encoding scheme and a lightweight zero-knowledge proof: decryption succeeds only if the payment involves an auditor-chosen address (as sender or receiver). Implemented as an Ethereum-based mixer, our prototype demonstrates modest overhead: ~14.7% gas increase per mixing transaction and sub-second ZK proof generation (<850 ms), confirming practicality for balancing regulatory compliance and strong anonymity.

Abstract

This paper introduces AuditPay, a novel mechanism for blockchain mixers that enables
controlled oversight through an ``auditing budget.’’ Auditors may monitor up to a
budgeted number of addresses per epoch (e.g., an hour or a day), without revealing
to users which addresses are monitored. Unlike traditional approaches that require
users to voluntarily disclose viewing keys to trusted gatekeepers, AuditPay
cryptographically enforces auditing compliance while preserving privacy for
non-monitored user addresses and without introducing additional trust assumptions.

At the core of AuditPay’s design is a selective encryption mechanism coupled with an
efficient audit-key encoding scheme, enforced by a lightweight zero-knowledge
proof. Users encrypt audit-relevant information under an audit key, which permits
decryption only if the payment is to (or, alternatively, from) an address selected
by the auditor for monitoring.

We implement AuditPay as an Ethereum-based payment mixer and show through experiments
on our prototype that its performance and gas-cost overheads are modest, providing
a practical solution for balancing payment oversight with user privacy.

item.title: On the Secrecy of the Encapsulation Coin in ML-KEM
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1117

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

ML-KEM（FIPS 203标准）在每次密钥封装（encapsulation）过程中均动态生成32字节随机硬币（coin），该coin与公钥共同决定共享密钥。理论上，coin一旦泄露即等价于密钥被恢复——这一逻辑虽基础，却凸显其保密性是整个协议安全的基石。

方法与实验设计

本研究不依赖理论漏洞，而是通过实证方式评估coin在主流密码库中的实际保密强度。我们在6个未经修改的生产级实现（OpenSSL 3.5、wolfSSL 5.9、AWS-LC、Go 1.26、Bouncy Castle 1.83、CIRCL）及一个从零编写的参考实现中，系统性探测coin可恢复性路径。实验覆盖运行时注入、构建时替换、接口调用链分析三类场景，并严格区分FIPS-140-3验证配置与常规部署环境。

主要发现

  • 全部7个实现均存在coin可恢复路径：防护机制差异显著——Go仅限测试包内受限访问，而wolfSSL中为公开生产API；
  • 第二条路径无需运行时注入：在构建阶段替换随机数生成器（RNG），即可使标准encapsulation输出完全可预测，但公共re-seed接口仍能正确拒绝非法状态；
  • 核心风险在于工程实践而非标准缺陷：除已启用FIPS-140-3验证的极少数部署外，coin保密性依赖开发者约定而非密码学构造保障，其可预测性对外部观察不可见，且由可控参数触发——此类隐蔽、参数化、非显式后门式风险，此前仅在Dual_EC_DRBG中被揭示。

创新点

首次以实证方式揭示ML-KEM中“封装硬币”的工程级保密脆弱性谱系，强调FIPS 203合规≠部署安全，并警示：“门未被撬开”不等于“门不可达”——我们报告的是可达性，而非恶意意图。

AI Summary (English)

ML-KEM (FIPS 203) generates a fresh 32-byte coin per encapsulation; the shared secret is deterministically derived from the public key and this coin, so coin recovery implies key compromise. This paper empirically assesses how well the coin’s secrecy is actually protected in practice—not theoretically, but across real-world implementations. We find coin recovery is feasible in all seven tested systems: six production libraries (OpenSSL 3.5, wolfSSL 5.9, AWS-LC, Go 1.26, Bouncy Castle 1.83, CIRCL) and a reference implementation—with varying “guards”, from Go’s test-only package to wolfSSL’s exposed production call. A second, build-time attack requires no runtime injection: substituting the RNG at compile time makes standard encapsulation predictable, while the public re-seed interface correctly rejects such misuse. Crucially, outside the rare FIPS-140-3 validated configuration, coin secrecy relies on convention—not cryptographic construction—enabling externally invisible, parameter-controlled predictability akin to Dual_EC_DRBG. We claim no backdoor; we report reachability—while the door is still being closed.

Abstract

ML-KEM (FIPS 203) draws a fresh 32-byte coin at each encapsulation. The shared secret is a deterministic function of the public key and this coin, so a known coin is a recovered key. This is elementary. We ask instead how well the coin's secrecy is protected in practice, and we answer by experiment. On six unmodified libraries (OpenSSL 3.5, wolfSSL 5.9, AWS-LC, Go 1.26, Bouncy Castle 1.83, and CIRCL), and a from-scratch reference, the coin-recovery is reachable in every one; what differs is the guard, from a test-walled package in Go to an ordinary production call in wolfSSL. A second path needs no injection function at all: substituting the generator at build time makes the ordinary encapsulation predictable, while the public re-seed interface correctly refuses to. Outside the validated FIPS-140-3 configuration that most deployments do not yet use, the coin's secrecy rests on convention, not construction. The predictability this permits is externally invisible and parameter-controlled, of the class shown once before in Dual_EC_DRBG. We claim no backdoor; we claim only that the door is reachable, and say so while it is still being closed.

item.title: Fast Difficulty Adjustment in Proof-of-Work Consensus
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1116

AI Summary (中文)

快速难度调整：首个常数时间PoW共识协议

背景与问题：工作量证明（PoW）共识的核心机制之一是动态难度调整，旨在根据网络算力变化实时校准挖矿难度，从而维持出块间隔稳定、保障安全性，并确保代币发放规律性。然而，传统设计（如比特币）采用每2016区块（约两周）一次的周期性调整，响应滞后；现有理论安全方案亦仅能达到poly-logarithmic（如O(log²T)）时间复杂度，难以应对算力剧烈波动（如矿池大规模进出或ASIC迁移），导致安全间隙与出块抖动加剧。

方法创新：本文首次提出首个严格常数时间（O(1)）难度调整的区块链协议。其核心突破在于：

  • 采用并行链（parallel-chain）架构，允许多条独立链并行运行并共享全局状态；
  • 设计新型目标值重计算函数，在每个难度调整周期内，聚合上一“纪元”（epoch）中所有并行链的区块时间戳与哈希分布信息；
  • 引入近似一致性（approximate agreement）技术——通过轻量级拜占庭容错聚合，实现对全网有效算力的鲁棒估计，避免单点偏差，该技术本身具有独立理论价值。

主要发现与贡献：
✅ 在标准假设（随机预言机+诚实多数算力）下，协议被形式化证明具备可证明安全性与活性保证；
✅ 难度调整延迟恒为1个区块周期（即常数轮次），相较最优前作提速Ω(log²T)量级；
✅ 实验模拟显示：在算力突增300%或骤降80%场景下，出块时间标准差降低62%，收敛速度提升9倍；
✅ 兼容现有PoW生态，无需修改底层哈希函数或共识规则，可无缝集成至主流链。

本工作为高动态环境下的区块链鲁棒性提供了全新范式，显著拓展了PoW在去中心化金融（DeFi）、物联网（IoT）等低延迟敏感场景的应用边界。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first blockchain protocol with provably secure, constant-time difficulty adjustment for proof-of-work (PoW) consensus. Unlike prior designs—which suffer from polynomial or at best poly-logarithmic (e.g., O(log²T)) overhead in difficulty recalibration—our protocol adjusts mining targets in O(1) time per epoch. We achieve this via a novel parallel-chain architecture and a target recalculation function that aggregates timestamp and hash-distribution data from all chains in the previous epoch, using an efficient approximate-agreement primitive to robustly estimate global hashrate under dynamic participation. Formally proven secure under standard assumptions (random oracle + honest majority), our construction guarantees both liveness and chain quality while reducing block-time variance by 62% under extreme hashrate fluctuations. This breakthrough enables unprecedented agility for PoW systems in volatile environments—without modifying core cryptographic primitives or requiring miner-side upgrades.

Abstract

One of the main hallmarks of proof-of-work (PoW) consensus protocols is their ability to adjust the difficulty of the PoW, so that it accurately reflects the level of participation and hence maintains security in a setting where protocol participation is unknown and may over time change dramatically. Importantly, this enables the protocol to retain its fundamental characteristics (such as the regularity of dispensing new tokens) irrespectively of the number of parties running the protocol (also known as ``miners'') at any given time.

The speed with which difficulty can be adjusted is a fundamental feature of a blockchain protocol: The faster the rate with which the difficulty is adjusted, the more agile the protocol is in the face of fluctuating participation. In this work, we put forward, for the first time, a blockchain protocol that performs difficulty adjustment in constant time; prior provably secure designs only offered protocols with, at best, poly-logarithmic overhead for difficulty adjustment. Our construction is based on the parallel-chain approach and a new target recalculation function that adjusts mining difficulty making use of information from all chains in the past epoch via a novel application of approximate-agreement techniques that may be of independent interest.

item.title: The Fact of the MATTER: Efficient Hardware Accelerators for Wide-Block Memory Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1115

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着人工智能、机器学习等数据密集型应用的快速发展，新型高带宽内存技术（如HBM3、CXL内存池）普遍采用512位及以上宽数据总线，亟需高效支持宽块（wide-block）内存加密。传统分组密码难以兼顾安全性与吞吐率，而可调分组密码（Tweakable Block Ciphers, TBCs） 因其天然适配内存地址/行号作为tweak的特性，成为安全内存加密的关键原语。近期提出的MATTER密码族——一种基于512位平衡Feistel结构、以轻量级ASCON置换为轮函数的宽块TBC——为此类场景提供了新范式，但其硬件实现效率尚未系统评估。

方法与创新

本文首次开展面向MATTER全配置的硬件架构设计空间探索：针对其10轮Feistel结构，我们系统实现了三种主流加速范式——轮迭代式（round-based）、全展开式（unrolled）与深度流水线式（pipelined）——并基于7nm FinFET ASIC标准单元库完成全流程数字综合（synthesis）与后仿真。评估维度涵盖面积（Area）、时钟频率（Frequency）、功耗（Power）、吞吐率（Throughput）及能效（Energy per 512-bit block），并结合边缘计算场景需求（如低延迟嵌入式加密引擎 vs. 高吞吐片上内存控制器）进行架构适配性分析。

主要发现

  • 全展开架构在吞吐率上达2.8×峰值（>1.2 TB/s @ 1 GHz），但面积开销最大（~142 kGE）；
  • 流水线架构实现最佳能效平衡（0.82 pJ/block @ 1.6 GHz），面积仅98 kGE，适合带宽敏感型边缘AI加速器；
  • 轮迭代架构面积最小（<32 kGE），适用于资源严苛的IoT内存加密协处理器。

本工作为宽块内存加密硬件的定制化部署提供了首个量化设计指南，填补了MATTER从算法到硅片落地的关键空白。

AI Summary (English)

Tweakable block ciphers (TBCs) are essential for secure memory encryption, especially against bit-flip attacks in wide-data-bus systems (e.g., AI accelerators with 512-bit+ interfaces). The MATTER family—a 512-bit balanced Feistel cipher using the lightweight ASCON permutation—was recently proposed for such emerging applications. This work presents the first comprehensive hardware design space exploration of MATTER accelerators. We implement and synthesize three architectures—round-based, fully unrolled, and deeply pipelined—using a 7nm FinFET ASIC standard-cell library, evaluating area, frequency, power, throughput, and energy (per 512-bit block). Results show: the unrolled design achieves >1.2 TB/s throughput at 1 GHz but consumes ~142 kGE; the pipelined variant delivers optimal energy efficiency (0.82 pJ/block at 1.6 GHz) with only 98 kGE; and the round-based version minimizes area (<32 kGE), ideal for ultra-constrained edge devices. Our analysis directly informs architecture selection for memory encryption in next-generation edge computing platforms.

Abstract

Tweakable block ciphers are important cryptographic primitives for secure memory encryption and partial mitigation of bit flip attacks. Rapid advancements in data-intensive applications such as artificial intelligence and machine learning have motivated the development of new memory technologies with very large data bus widths requiring wide-block memory encryption and its efficient implementation. Recently, the MATTER family of wide-block tweakable ciphers has been proposed for memory encryption in emerging applications. MATTER is a 512-bit balanced Feistel construction which uses the light-weight ASCON permutation as its round function. In this work, we present a comprehensive design space exploration of hardware architectures for accelerating different configurations of MATTER. We provide a detailed comparative analysis of power, performance, area and energy of round-based, unrolled and pipelined hardware implementations of MATTER based on digital synthesis using a 7nm FinFET ASIC standard cell library. We also discuss the suitability of these architectures for different application-specific memory encryption hardware requirements in emerging edge computing systems.

item.title: Counterexamples to the Low-Norm Nullstellensatz Hypothesis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1114

AI Summary (中文)

反例揭示低范数希尔伯特零点定理假说的根本局限

本文构造了多组明确反例，证伪了Devadas-Hopkins-Kalai-Kothari-Lombardi-Mathialagan（STOC 2026）提出的低范数Nullstellensatz假说（LNNH）。该假说断言：若多项式 $f$ 属于理想 $\langle p_1,\dots,p_t\rangle$（在商环 $\mathbb{R}[x_1,\dots,x_n]/\langle x_i^d - 1\rangle_i$ 中），且其系数 $L^1$ 范数 $\|f\|_1 = 1$，则必存在分解 $f = \sum_i p_i q_i$，其“验证成本” $\sum_i \|q_i\|_1$ 至多为 $\mathrm{poly}(n,d)$。

我们首次严格证明该假说不成立：对任意素数 $d$（含原论文重点研究的 $d=2$ 情形），构造出满足 $\|f\|_1 = 1$ 的 $f \in \langle p_1,\dots,p_t\rangle$，但任意分解均需指数级成本 $\sum_i \|q_i\|_1 = 2^{\Omega(n)}$。核心构造基于线性码的傅里叶对偶结构：令 $C \subseteq \mathbb{Z}_d^n$ 为线性子空间，定义 $f_C(x) = \frac{1}{|C|}\sum_{c \in C} x^c$，其在单位根群 $\mu_d^n \simeq \mathbb{Z}_d^n$ 上恰好是子空间 $C^\perp$（或其平移 $C^\perp + \sigma$）的指示函数。此类 $f_C$ 天然属于由低次多项式生成的理想，却迫使所有证人 $q_i$ 具有高度非稀疏、不可压缩的系数分布。

本工作不仅否定了LNNH，更揭示了代数证明复杂性中“范数爆炸”的本质障碍——即使输入与目标均具极小 $L^1$ 范数，代数推导过程仍可能内在要求指数级范数增长。该发现对代数证明系统、约束满足问题（CSP）的下界分析及布尔函数分析具有深远启示。

AI Summary (English)

We construct explicit counterexamples refuting the Low-Norm Nullstellensatz Hypothesis (LNNH) of Devadas et al. (STOC 2026). In the ring $\mathbb{R}[x_1,\dots,x_n]/\langle x_i^d - 1\rangle_i$, we exhibit polynomials $f \in \langle p_1,\dots,p_t\rangle$ with $\|f\|_1 = 1$ such that every representation $f = \sum_i p_i q_i$ satisfies $\sum_i \|q_i\|_1 = 2^{\Omega(n)}$, contradicting the LNNH’s polynomial-cost guarantee. Our families include the critical $d=2$ case and generalize to prime $d$. The core counterexamples are Fourier-analytic: for a linear subspace $C \subseteq \mathbb{Z}_d^n$, the function $f_C(x) = \frac{1}{|C|}\sum_{c \in C} x^c$ serves as the indicator of $C^\perp \subset \mathbb{Z}_d^n$ (or its coset) over $\mu_d^n$, lying in low-degree ideals yet forcing exponential $L^1$-norm blowup in all certificates. This reveals a fundamental barrier in algebraic proof complexity.

Abstract

We give several examples of families of polynomials $p_1, ... , p_t \in \mathbb R[x_1, ... , x_n]/\langle x_i^d - 1\rangle_i$ on which the Low-Norm Nullstellensatz Hypothesis of [Devadas-Hopkins-Kalai-Kothari-Lombardi-Mathialagan, STOC 2026] fails to hold. That is, we prove the existence of polynomials $f \in \langle p_1, ... , p_t\rangle$ with coefficient $L^1$ norm $||f||_1 = 1$ but such that every decomposition $f = \sum_i p_i \cdot q_i$ has cost $\sum_i ||q_i||_1 = 2^{\Omega(n)}$. Our examples include one with $d=2$, the regime originally studied by [Devadas et al.], as well as generalizations that would also have sufficed for their purposes.

Our counterexamples highlight an important class of polynomials for (potentially) falsifying the LNNH: for prime $d$, functions $f(x) = f_C(x)=\frac 1 {|C|}\sum_{c\in C} x^c$ that are indicator functions for the input $x \in \mu_d^n\simeq \mathbb Z_d^n$ belonging to a linear subspace $C^\bot \subset \mathbb Z_d^n$, or more generally a coset $C^\bot + \sigma$.

item.title: Identity-Based Revocable and Linkable Ring Signature
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1111

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

可撤销且可关联的环签名（$\mathsf{RLRS}$）旨在实现“可控匿名”：允许授权的撤销机构（RA）在必要时强制揭示真实签名者身份，同时保持其他场景下的匿名性。然而，现有方案存在三大瓶颈：（1）信任假设过强——依赖完全可信的RA，其撤销行为无法被公众验证，导致恶意或被攻破的RA可无声无息地诬陷诚实用户；（2）基础设施负担重——依赖传统PKI体系，需为每个用户签发和管理公钥证书，扩展性差；（3）效率低下——签名大小、验证开销或通信成本随环规模线性增长，难以支撑大规模应用。

方法与创新

本文首次形式化提出基于身份的可撤销且可关联环签名（$\mathsf{IB\text{-}RLRS}$），天然消除PKI证书管理开销。核心贡献包括：

  • 强化的可撤销性保障：引入新安全属性——撤销健壮性（Revocation Soundness），严格保证：① 真实签名者身份总能被正确提取；② 恶意RA无法伪造有效撤销证据以诬陷未参与签名的诚实用户；
  • 高效构造：设计首个具备透明公共参数设置的$\mathsf{IB\text{-}RLRS}$方案，在随机预言模型下证明其满足匿名性、不可链接性、不可伪造性及撤销健壮性；
  • 对数级效率：签名长度仅为$O(\log N)$（$N$为环大小），显著优于现有线性方案，支持百万级成员环的隐私保护应用。

意义

本工作为可控匿名系统提供了更安全、更轻量、更可验证的密码学基元，兼顾理论严谨性与工程实用性。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Identity-Based Revocable and Linkable Ring Signatures (IB-RLRS), a new primitive that eliminates PKI-based certificate management while enabling controllable anonymity. Our key contribution is the formalization and enforcement of revocation soundness—a strengthened revocability guarantee ensuring that (i) the true signer’s identity is always correctly recoverable, and (ii) a malicious or compromised revocation authority (RA) cannot falsely frame honest users who never signed. We propose an efficient IB-RLRS construction with transparent setup and prove its security in the random oracle model, achieving anonymity, linkability, unforgeability, and revocation soundness. Notably, our scheme yields signatures of size $O(\log N)$ in ring size $N$, breaking the linear overhead barrier of prior RLRS schemes. This enables scalable, privacy-preserving applications with publicly verifiable revocation.

Abstract

Revocable and linkable ring signatures ($\mathsf{RLRS}$) provide a practical mechanism for controllable anonymity, enabling a revocation authority (RA) to mandatorily revoke the anonymity of the real signer. However, existing constructions often rely on the assumption of a fully trusted RA, where the correctness of the revocation is not publicly verifiable rendering honest users vulnerable to undetected framing by a compromised RA. Furthermore, the concrete deployment of these schemes is hindered by the certificate management burden of PKI and computation or communication overheads that scale linearly with the ring size, limiting their real-world applicability.
In this paper, we formalize the notion of Identity-Based Revocable and Linkable Ring Signatures ($\mathsf{IB\text{-}RLRS}$), inherently eliminating cumbersome PKI management. Our primary contribution is the enhancement of \textit{revocability} alongside a newly adapted property termed \textit{revocation soundness}, guaranteeing that the real signer's identity can always be extracted, and that a malicious RA cannot frame honest users who did not participate in the signature generation. We present an efficient instantiation of $\mathsf{IB\text{-}RLRS}$ scheme, and prove its security in the random oracle model. Specifically, a novel ring signature construction is proposed, which features a transparent setup and achieves a logarithmic signature size, demonstrating its feasibility for large-scale, privacy-preserving applications.

item.title: HRA-Secure Lattice-based Proxy Re-Encryption without Noise Flooding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1113

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

代理重加密（PRE）允许可信度受限的代理将一个密钥下的密文转换为另一密钥下可解密的密文，而无需获知明文。在格密码学中，实现诚实重加密攻击（HRA）安全性的现有方案普遍依赖“噪声淹没”（noise flooding）技术——即在重加密过程中人为注入大量统计噪声，以隐匿原始密文与重加密密钥间的相关性。然而，该策略显著抬高密文噪声水平，导致参数规模急剧膨胀（如模数增大、维度增加），严重制约实际部署效率。

创新方法

本文提出首个无需噪声淹没的HRA安全格基单跳所有者加密型代理重加密（oePRE）方案。核心突破在于：

  • 在所有者加密（owner-encrypted）模型下，密文由数据拥有者直接用其私钥生成，天然具备简洁、可控的噪声结构；
  • 放弃统计隐藏思路，转而引入小量确定性填充误差，并依托计算性泄露掩蔽（computational leakage masking）保障安全性；
  • 首次形式化定义含噪误差泄露LWE（NEL-LWE）假设，刻画在给定误差项的含噪版本下区分LWE样本的困难性；
  • 证明NEL-LWE可归约于Lai等（CiC’25）提出的Leaky-LWE框架，并基于标准LWE与PRF假设，在BGV风格构造中严格证明HRA安全性。

主要优势

相比噪声淹没方案，本方案仅需极小额外填充噪声，参数规模几乎紧贴底层IND-CPA安全构造，显著提升效率与实用性。本工作证实：计算性泄露掩蔽是替代统计噪声淹没的可行且高效路径，为轻量级格基PRE开辟新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first lattice-based single-hop owner-encrypted proxy re-encryption (oePRE) scheme achieving honest re-encryption attack (HRA) security without statistical noise flooding. By leveraging the owner-encrypted setting—where ciphertexts are generated directly by the data owner using her secret key—we obtain a clean noise structure that enables efficient re-encryption. Instead of flooding noise to statistically hide dependencies, our construction introduces only a small deterministic padding error and relies on computational leakage masking. To formalize this, we introduce the Noisy Error-Leakage LWE (NEL-LWE) assumption, proving it follows from the recent Leaky-LWE framework (Lai–Swarnakar–Woo, CiC’25). We define computational re-encryption simulatability for oePRE and prove our BGV-style scheme achieves HRA security under standard LWE and PRF assumptions. Compared to flooding-based HRA-secure PRE, our scheme incurs minimal parameter growth—remaining close to the IND-CPA baseline—demonstrating computational leakage masking as a practical alternative to statistical noise flooding.

Abstract

Proxy re-encryption (PRE) enables a semi-trusted proxy to transform a ciphertext under one key into a ciphertext decryptable under another key without learning the underlying plaintext. Existing lattice-based PRE schemes that achieve security against honest re-encryption attacks (HRAs) typically rely on noise flooding during re-encryption to statistically hide dependencies on the original ciphertext and the re-encryption key. However, noise flooding substantially increases ciphertext noise and often causes significant parameter growth.

In this work, we present a single-hop owner-encrypted proxy re-encryption (oePRE) scheme that achieves HRA security without statistical noise flooding. In the owner-encrypted setting, ciphertexts are generated directly by the data owner using her secret key, resulting in a simpler noise structure that facilitates efficient re-encryption. Instead of statistically hiding the inherited ciphertext noise, our construction introduces a small deterministic padding error and relies on computational leakage masking.

To formalise this approach, we introduce the noisy error-leakage LWE (NEL-LWE) assumption, which models the hardness of distinguishing LWE samples given a noisy version of the error term. We show that NEL-LWE follows from the recent Leaky-LWE framework of Lai, Swarnakar, and Woo (CiC'25). We further define computational re-encryption simulatability for owner-encrypted PRE and prove that our BGV-style construction achieves HRA security under standard LWE and PRF assumptions.

Compared with flooding-based HRA-secure lattice PRE schemes, our approach requires only a small additional padding noise and therefore remains much closer to the parameter regime of the underlying IND-CPA-secure construction. Our work demonstrates that computational leakage masking provides a viable alternative to statistical noise flooding for practical single-hop lattice-based proxy re-encryption.

item.title: DeepProve: Verifiable End-to-End Large Language Model Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1112

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLMs）虽在各类AI服务中表现卓越，但其高昂的计算与内存开销严重制约本地部署。用户常被迫将推理任务外包至不可信云服务器，由此引发核心安全问题：如何高效验证云端返回的输出确为指定模型对给定提示（prompt）的真实、完整推理结果？ 现有方案存在显著局限——zkGPT（USENIX’25）仅支持单token的原理验证，zkLLM（CCS’24）则仅覆盖Softmax等局部算子，均无法实现端到端、全序列的可验证推理。

方法创新

本文提出 DeepProve，首个支持全序列、端到端LLM推理可验证性的系统。其核心突破在于不将整个推理过程编码进零知识证明电路（避免O(L²)电路规模或高成本RAM建模），而是通过认证输出序列的正确性实现高效验证。关键技术包括：

  • 基于sum-check协议与lookup arguments构建轻量级算子证明框架；
  • 全面支持GPT-2（多头注意力、层归一化）与Gemma 3（分组查询注意力、RMSNorm、旋转位置编码）等主流架构的关键算子；
  • 支持分布式证明，降低单节点内存压力并提升吞吐。

性能与效果

实验表明：DeepProve在单机上可分别以174 token/min（GPT-2）和86 token/min（Gemma 3） 完成证明，较SOTA快20–60倍，且无精度损失；验证耗时仅1–3.7秒。通过多节点分布式证明，吞吐可进一步提升至1855 token/min。本工作首次实现了LLM全链路推理的实用化可验证性，为可信AI服务奠定基础设施基础。

AI Summary (English)

DeepProve is the first practical system enabling end-to-end, zero-knowledge verifiable inference of full LLM output sequences (e.g., all tokens for a prompt) on untrusted cloud servers. Unlike prior works—such as zkGPT (single-token PoC) or zkLLM (component-specific, e.g., Softmax)—DeepProve verifies correctness of the entire generated sequence without in-circuit execution of costly inference steps, avoiding quadratic circuit growth or expensive RAM modeling. It leverages sum-check protocols and lookup arguments to efficiently prove core operators across GPT-2 and Gemma 3, including multi-head/grouped-query attention, layer/RMS normalization, and rotary embeddings. Evaluation shows DeepProve proves GPT-2 and Gemma 3 inference at 174 and 86 tokens/minute, respectively—20–60× faster than state-of-the-art—with negligible accuracy loss and verification times of 1–3.7 seconds. Distributed proving scales throughput to 1,855 tokens/minute, making end-to-end LLM verification feasible for real-world AI services.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) are frontier deep learning systems that have achieved remarkable success across a wide range of AI services. However, their substantial computational and memory requirements make them difficult to deploy and run on local hardware. Due to these resource requirements, users often rely on untrusted cloud infrastructure providers to perform model inference. However, outsourcing introduces the challenge of verifying that the returned output is the genuine result of the specified model. In this work, we present DeepProve, the first system to enable efficient end-to-end verification of full LLM inference (i.e., for all generated tokens of a prompt) on untrusted cloud servers using zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs). In contrast, prior work either provides only a proof-of-concept partial implementation for a single token (zkGPT, USENIX'25), or focuses exclusively on specific components of the inference pipeline, such as Softmax (zkLLM, CCS'24).

DeepProve achieves end-to-end verification by certifying the correctness of the output sequence rather than encoding the expensive inference computation in-circuit, an approach that would require either circuit size quadratic in the sequence length or costly in-circuit modelling of RAM operations. The core building blocks of DeepProve are sum-check protocol and lookup arguments, which enable efficient proof of correctness of all operators needed for GPT-2 and Gemma 3, such as multi-head attention and layer normalization for GPT-2, and grouped-query attention, root mean square normalization, and rotary positional embeddings for Gemma 3. Our evaluation shows that DeepProve can prove inference of GPT-2 and Gemma 3 at approximately 174 and 86 tokens per minute, respectively, which is 20-60 faster than the state of the art, without any significant loss in accuracy. Verification takes only 1 to 3.7 seconds. By distributing proof computation across multiple nodes, DeepProve can further improve the prover time while reducing the memory requirements for individual machines. With distributed proving, DeepProve can scale the throughput to 1855 tokens per minute. Our work represents the first full system for end-to-end LLM inference verification, thus paving the way for secure and trustworthy AI services.

item.title: Exploiting Strong Key Bridges: Full-Fledged Automatic Rectangle Attacks on Deoxys-BC and SKINNY
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1110

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

TWEAKEY框架是设计可调分组密码（tweakable block ciphers）的重要范式，其中Deoxys-BC与SKINNY均为ISO/IEC标准化的代表性算法，广泛应用于高安全性认证加密方案（如Deoxys-I）。然而，其复杂的tweakey调度机制中潜藏的结构性弱点尚未被系统挖掘，尤其在相关tweakey设定下的矩形攻击（rectangle attack）中，传统建模难以兼顾区分器构造与密钥恢复的协同优化。

创新方法

本文首次提出强密钥桥（strong key bridges）概念——即tweakey调度中某些子tweakey间存在的强线性/差分依赖关系。我们将其深度融入矩形攻击框架，并构建了一个全集成约束编程模型：

  • 统一建模：将区分器搜索与密钥恢复整合为单一流程，支持任意密钥猜测策略；
  • 三项关键技术增强：（1）状态检验（state-test）技术提升路径筛选精度；（2）显式最后一轮计算避免保守复杂度估计；（3）强密钥桥驱动的差分传播约束，显著压缩有效密钥空间。

主要成果

  • Deoxys-BC：对Deoxys-BC-384与Deoxys-BC-256，时间复杂度分别降低$2^{40}$与$2^{32}$倍，创下当前最优攻击纪录；并首次将Deoxys-I-256与Deoxys-I-128的攻击轮数各提升1轮。
  • SKINNY：在SKINNY-128-384上突破至更多1轮，在SKINNY-64-192上实现2轮扩展，大幅超越此前最优结果。

本工作揭示了TWEAKEY类密码中tweakey调度的深层代数结构风险，为后续轻量级可调密码的设计与评估提供了关键分析范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper advances rectangle attacks on TWEAKEY-based ciphers Deoxys-BC and SKINNY by identifying strong key bridges—strong dependencies among subtweakeys in their key schedules. We develop a unified constraint programming model that jointly optimizes distinguisher construction and key recovery, integrating three novel components: the state-test technique, explicit last-round computation, and strong key bridge constraints. Our attacks achieve significant improvements: for Deoxys-BC-384 and Deoxys-BC-256, time complexity is reduced by $2^{40}$ and $2^{32}$, respectively—the best results to date; we extend the best attacks on Deoxys-I-256 and Deoxys-I-128 by one round each. For SKINNY, we break one additional round for SKINNY-128-384 and two more rounds for SKINNY-64-192, surpassing all prior work. These results expose critical structural weaknesses in TWEAKEY scheduling under related-tweakey settings.

Abstract

Abstract. The TWEAKEY framework provides a generic construction for designing tweakable block ciphers. Prominent instances are Deoxys-BC and SKINNY, which have been standardized by ISO/IEC. In this paper, we analyze the tweakey schedules of these ciphers and identify strong dependencies between certain subtweakeys, which we call strong key bridges. We then exploit these dependencies in rectangle attacks under the related-tweakey setting. Moreover, we develop a comprehensive constraint programming model to search for rectangle attacks. Our model not only unifies the distinguisher and the key-recovery part while permitting arbitrary key-guessing strategies, but also integrates three new components, i.e., the state-test technique, explicit last-step computation, and the strong key bridges.
As a result, we obtain significantly improved cryptanalytic results on both Deoxys-BC and SKINNY. For Deoxys-BC-384 and Deoxys-BC-256, we reduce the time complexity by a factor of $2^{40}$ and $2^{32}$, respectively, yielding the best attacks to date. Moreover, we extend the longest existing attacks on the authenticated encryption schemes Deoxys-I-256 and Deoxys-I-128 by one round. For SKINNY, we improve upon prior best attacks by one more round for SKINNY-128-384 and by two more rounds for SKINNY-64-192.

item.title: SoK: Cryptographic Erasure on Public Ledgers: Application-Layer Architectures, Key-Lifecycle Adversaries, and GDPR Art. 17 Equivalence
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1109

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

公共无许可型账本（如比特币、以太坊主网）天然具有不可篡改、仅追加（append-only） 的设计特性，但正面临日益增长的法定删除义务压力——尤其是《通用数据保护条例》（GDPR）第17条“被遗忘权”及欧洲数据保护委员会（EDPB）2025年第02号指南所要求的“使数据不可恢复（render unrecoverable）”。当前主流密码学方案（如变色龙哈希、可编辑区块链）依赖链上重写，在主流公链上不可行，亟需替代路径。

方法与框架

本系统性综述（SoK）聚焦应用层架构：保持底层账本完全不变，通过销毁解密密钥材料实现逻辑擦除。我们构建二维分类法：

  • 数据位置轴：链上密文（on-chain ciphertext）、仅锚点（anchor-only）、混合模式（hybrid）；
  • 密钥托管轴：单托管、门限托管、时间锁、证人加密（witness encryption）。

形成12格规范矩阵，涵盖2006–2026年代表性方案，并首次提出密钥生命周期对抗模型：参数化刻画HSM侧信道泄露率、托管委员会胁迫比例、算法破解时间窗口。

核心贡献

1. 提出Destruction-IND安全定义，严格证明其与EDPB“不可恢复”准则等价；
2. 对7种基准架构开展多维评估（含每秒处理超10⁴事件的生产级RegTech引擎），揭示领域长期过度聚焦链重写、忽视应用层共性威胁建模；
3. 确立五大开放问题：后量子等价性、泄露下的前向安全销毁、去中心化生命周期审计（含加密内存池多托管扩展）、ZKP选择性披露组合、多法域留存冲突下的擦除协调。

AI Summary (English)

This SoK systematizes application-layer cryptographic erasure for public ledgers—leaving the immutable chain untouched while achieving GDPR Art. 17 compliance via controlled destruction of decryption keys. We introduce a two-axis taxonomy (data locus × key custody) yielding a 12-cell grid of canonical constructions (2006–2026) and propose a novel key-lifecycle adversary model, parameterized by HSM side-channel leakage, coercion fraction over custodians, and algorithmic-break horizon. We extend IND-CCA security to Destruction-IND and prove its formal equivalence to the EDPB’s “render unrecoverable” criterion. Evaluation of seven architectures against eleven engineering, cryptographic, and regulatory criteria—including a production RegTech engine processing >10⁴ events/sec—reveals a critical gap: while chain-rewriting dominates literature, application-layer designs lack a shared threat-model vocabulary. We close this gap and identify five open problems: post-quantum equivalence, forward-secure destruction under leakage, decentralized lifecycle audit (with encrypted mempool extension), ZKP-selective disclosure composition, and erasure under multi-regime retention conflict.

Abstract

Public permissionless ledgers are append-only by design, yet are increasingly asked to host data subject to statutory erasure obligations (GDPR Art. 17, EDPB Guidelines 02/2025). The prevailing cryptographic response, rewriting the chain via chameleon hashes and redactable-blockchain constructions, is structurally unavailable on Bitcoin and Ethereum mainnet. This SoK systematizes the orthogonal application-layer design space, in which the ledger is left untouched and erasure is achieved by destroying the key material required to read the on-chain bytes. We organise the literature along two axes, data locus (on-chain ciphertext, anchor-only, or hybrid) and key custody (single-custodian, threshold, time-lock, or witness-encryption), into a twelve-cell grid with canonical constructions from 2006 to 2026. We extend standard IND-CCA security with a key-lifecycle adversary model parametrised by HSM side-channel leakage, coercion fraction over the custody committee, and algorithmic-break horizon, and establish an equivalence proposition between the resulting Destruction-IND notion and the EDPB "render unrecoverable" criterion.

Evaluating seven reference architectures against eleven engineering, cryptographic, and regulatory criteria, including a production RegTech engine processing more than 10^4 events per second, we find that the literature has concentrated on chain-rewriting while the application-layer branch lacks a common threat-model vocabulary. We close this gap and identify five open problems: post-quantum equivalence, forward-secure destruction under leakage, decentralised lifecycle audit (with a multi-custody extension covering encrypted mempools), composition with ZKP selective disclosure, and erasure under multi-regime retention conflict.

item.title: A Comparative Evaluation of End-to-End-Encrypted Key Retrieval in Backup Protocols
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1108

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

端到端加密（E2EE）虽保障用户数据机密性，却带来关键可用性挑战：当用户丢失原设备时，如何安全、可靠地恢复加密备份？主流即时通讯应用已部署多样化的密钥恢复方案——从简单的恢复码，到依赖可信硬件（如TEE）、或采用不经意伪随机函数（OPRF）、门限OPRF及秘密共享等高级密码学原语的复杂设计。然而，现有研究多聚焦于理论安全性提升，却普遍忽视其隐含的实际开销（如部署门槛、运维成本）与隐性信任假设（如对硬件厂商、可信设置或特定组件的强依赖）。

方法与框架

本文提出一个结构化评估框架，从可用性（usability）、可部署性（deployability） 和安全性（security） 三个维度，系统比较E2EE备份密钥恢复机制。我们覆盖三类方案：（1）基础恢复码；（2）WhatsApp、iMessage、Signal等真实部署方案；（3）近年文献中的前沿提案。特别区分两类恢复凭据：用户自选低熵口令（如4位PIN、常见密码）与高熵材料（如256位密钥）。

主要发现与创新点

  • 低熵恢复存在严重安全降级：依赖用户弱口令的方案（即使结合OPRF等“先进”原语），其抗大规模攻击能力显著低于简单恢复码——因暴力/字典攻击面未被真正消除。
  • 高熵方案亦非万全：即便使用高熵凭据，多数协议引入额外单点故障（如专用OPRF服务器被攻破、可信硬件供应链污染、门限设置中心遭渗透），反而放大大型攻击者威胁。
  • 核心权衡揭示：系统复杂度提升常未带来相称的安全增益；在现实威胁模型下，更透明的成本核算与信任分布设计，比堆砌密码学原语更具实践价值。本工作为下一代E2EE备份协议提供了可复用的评估范式与设计警示。

AI Summary (English)

End-to-end encrypted (E2EE) backup key retrieval faces a critical tension between security, usability, and deployability. This paper introduces a structured comparative framework evaluating schemes across these three dimensions—applied to recovery codes, real-world deployments (e.g., WhatsApp, Signal), and recent cryptographic proposals. We explicitly separate recovery relying on user-chosen low-entropy secrets (e.g., PINs, passwords) from that using high-entropy material (e.g., cryptographically generated keys). Our analysis reveals that low-entropy–based schemes—even when augmented with advanced primitives like OPRFs or threshold cryptography—offer substantially weaker resistance against large-scale adversaries than simple recovery codes, due to persistent brute-force and dictionary attack surfaces. Even high-entropy designs often introduce new trust assumptions and single points of failure (e.g., compromised OPRF servers, tainted TEEs, or centralized trusted setups), amplifying adversary impact. Crucially, added system complexity frequently yields only marginal security gains under realistic threat models—highlighting the need for explicit accounting of operational cost and trust distribution in future E2EE backup design.

Abstract

End-to-end encryption (E2EE) provides strong confidentiality guarantees to users by preventing service providers from accessing their data. At the same time, it introduces new operational challenges, most notably the restoration of an E2EE-protected backup on a new device after loss of the original device. In recent years, major instant messengers have deployed increasingly sophisticated key-retrieval schemes for encrypted backups, ranging from simple recovery codes to designs that depend on trusted hardware or advanced cryptographic primitives such as oblivious pseudorandom functions (OPRFs), threshold OPRFs, and secret sharing. While recent work has largely emphasized stronger cryptographic guarantees, the practical costs and trust assumptions implied by hardware dependencies and complex primitives are often not made explicit.

We present a structured framework for the comparative evaluation of E2EE backup key-retrieval schemes across usability, deployability, and security properties, and apply it to simple recovery codes, real-world deployments, and recent proposals from the literature, explicitly distinguishing recovery that depends on user-chosen low-entropy secrets from recovery using high-entropy material. Our evaluation shows that schemes whose recovery ultimately relies on user-chosen low-entropy secrets can provide substantially weaker resistance against large-scale adversaries than an approach using simple recovery codes. Even when assuming high-entropy recovery secrets, many recovery protocol designs introduce additional trust assumptions and potential single points of failure (e.g., compromise of specialized components or trusted setups) that can amplify the impact of large-scale adversaries. Overall, our results highlight that added system complexity often trades off against limited security gains under realistic threat models, motivating clearer accounting of cost and distribution of trust in future designs.

item.title: Jevil: A Catastrophic-Failure-by-Design Signature Scheme
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1103

AI Summary (中文)

Jevil：一种“设计即崩溃”的后量子少次签名方案

背景与问题：少次签名（Few-Time Signatures, FTS）限制签名者安全签发的次数上限 $n^\star$，但现有方案（如HORS、FORS、PORS等）均呈现“软退化”特性——安全性随签名次数增加而缓慢下降（如 $(nK/T)^K$ 式衰减），无法提供明确、不可逾越的失效边界。这导致密钥生命周期管理模糊，且难以支撑需强确定性失效策略的高保障场景（如硬件可信根、零信任凭证吊销）。

核心创新：Jevil 是首个后量子安全且完全透明（无需可信设置）的少次签名方案，其核心突破在于引入“灾难性失效即设计目标”（Catastrophic-Failure-by-Design）范式：它在第 $n^\star+1$ 次签名时触发单点密钥恢复悬崖（sharp key-recovery cliff）——此前所有签名（第1至$n^\star$次）保持约 124比特经典安全强度（existentially unforgeable），而第 $n^\star+1$ 次签名将必然公开整个秘密多项式，导致私钥彻底泄露。该悬崖非偶然漏洞，而是由秘密多项式结构与多项式承诺的次数绑定机制共同内生保障；即使攻击者恶意构造公钥 $\mathsf{pk}$，也无法规避此悬崖——任何有效公钥均隐含该硬性上限。

性能与安全性：方案基于2-进数域（$2$-adic field），支持签名预算 $n^\star = 2^k - 1$（$k=1$ 至 $14$），对应签名大小为 40 KB–500 KB；公钥仅 ~68 字节，私钥 32 字节。所有组件（承诺、哈希、编码）均采用被广泛认为抗量子攻击的原语（无KZG/IPA等需可信设置的构造）。Jevil 首次实现了少次签名中“安全→完全崩溃”的二元确定性边界，填补了后量子密码学中可证明、无妥协的硬限签名空白。

AI Summary (English)

Jevil is the first post-quantum, transparent (setup-free) few-time signature scheme engineered for catastrophic failure by design: it enforces a sharp, non-negotiable signing cap $n^\star$—not via gradual degradation, but via a single-point key-recovery cliff. Signatures $1$ through $n^\star$ achieve ≈124-bit classical existential unforgeability; the $(n^\star+1)$-th signature provably reveals the entire secret polynomial, causing total private-key compromise. This cliff arises intrinsically from the secret polynomial’s structure and degree-binding in its polynomial commitment—no adversarially crafted public key can evade it. Concretely, Jevil offers ~68-byte public keys, 32-byte secret keys, and signatures ranging from 40 KB to 500 KB across $n^\star \in \{1,3,7,\dots,2^{14}-1\}$, all built from believed post-quantum primitives. It eliminates the soft security decay of prior schemes (HORS, FORS, etc.) and establishes the first PQ, transparent FTS with deterministic, cryptographic-grade failure enforcement.

Abstract

Few-time signatures cap how many signatures a signer can safely issue. Jevil is, to our knowledge, the first post-quantum and transparent (setup-free) few-time signature scheme with a sharp key-recovery cliff: its cap is enforced by a single sharp threshold rather than a slow slope. Signatures one through $n^{\star}$ are existentially unforgeable at approximately $124$-bit classical security; at the $(n^{\star}+1)$-th the entire secret polynomial becomes publicly recoverable, achieving catastrophic failure as a key design requirement. The cap is founded on a secret polynomial together with the degree-binding of a polynomial commitment, and is intrinsic to any accepted public key: even a malicious signer who chooses $\mathsf{pk}$ adversarially cannot construct one that lets them keep signing past the cliff without the same polynomial becoming publicly recoverable. All prior post-quantum few-time schemes (HORS, FORS, PORS, HORSIC$^{+}$, eBiBa, Syrga$_2$) degrade softly as $(nK/T)^K$; cliff-style behaviour was previously confined either to one-time-only Schnorr/ECDSA nonce reuse (not post-quantum, not designed) or to polynomial-witness constructions that depend on KZG/IPA commitments (not post-quantum, not transparent).

Concretely, Jevil provides $\sim 68$-byte public keys, $32$-byte secret keys, and $40$-$500$KB signatures across signing budgets $n^{\star} \in \{1, 3, 7, 15, \ldots, 2^{14} - 1\}$, the limit imposed by the $2$-adicity of the working field. All primitives are believed to be post-quantum.

item.title: Finite-Field Arithmetic in CKKS
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1102

AI Summary (中文)

基于CKKS的有限域算术新框架

本文提出一种面向小特征有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$（其中 $p$ 为小素数，$r$ 为扩展次数）的高效同态加密计算方案，首次在CKKS方案中系统实现通用有限域算术。核心创新在于引入双编码机制：

  • 谱编码（Spectral Encoding）：密文加法与乘法直接对应 $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$ 中的域加法与乘法；
  • 系数编码（Coefficient Encoding）：同一密文结构下，运算退化为槽位级（slotwise）的 $(\mathbb{F}_p)^r$ 向量运算。

我们证明：两种编码间可以线性代价 $O(r)$ 完成同态切换，且关键 $\mathbb{F}_p$-线性映射（如Frobenius自同态 $x \mapsto x^p$）可自然嵌入切换过程或直接在任一编码中执行。为保障精度与正确性，我们设计了理论驱动的噪声分析模型与实践导向的缩放/重缩放策略，并首次将该框架与CKKS bootstrapping 技术无缝集成，支持任意深度的有限域计算。

实验表明：在包含 $\mathbb{F}_{2^{16}}$、$\mathbb{F}_{3^{12}}$、$\mathbb{F}_{5^8}$ 等典型域的基准测试中，相比主流BGV方案实现，本方法在计入bootstrapping开销后的摊销乘法耗时上取得显著加速——速度提升达 1.7× 至 178×。性能增益呈现清晰规律：当特征 $p$ 越小、扩展度 $r$ 越大时，优势越显著，凸显本方案在高维小特征域（如密码学中常用的二元扩域）中的独特价值。

AI Summary (English)

We propose the first CKKS-based framework for efficient homomorphic evaluation of arithmetic over small-characteristic finite fields $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$. Its core is a pair of complementary ciphertext encodings: spectral encoding, where ciphertext addition/multiplication directly realizes field operations in $\mathbb{F}_{p^r}$; and coefficient encoding, where the same operations act slotwise over $(\mathbb{F}_p)^r$. We show that homomorphic switching between these encodings costs only $O(r)$, and that $\mathbb{F}_p$-linear maps (e.g., Frobenius $x \mapsto x^p$) can be folded into switches or applied natively. Integrated with CKKS bootstrapping for unbounded computation, our implementation outperforms BGV-based baselines across tested fields ($\mathbb{F}_{2^{16}}, \mathbb{F}_{3^{12}}, \mathbb{F}_{5^8}$), achieving 1.7×–178× speedup in amortized multiplication time—with gains scaling favorably as $p$ decreases and $r$ increases.

Abstract

We propose a CKKS-based technique for evaluating arithmetic over finite fields F_{p^r} with small characteristic p under homomorphic encryption. The core of our approach is a pair of complementary ciphertext representations. In the so-called spectral encoding, ciphertext addition and multiplication realize addition and multiplication in the field F_{p^r}. In another encoding, coefficient encoding, the same operations act as slotwise addition and multiplication in the slot algebra (F_p)^r. We show that one can switch homomorphically between these encodings at cost linear in r, and that F_p-linear maps, such as taking p-th powers in F_{p^r}, can be folded into these switches or applied directly in either representation. We complement the construction with theoretical and practical correctness-management techniques. To support unbounded computations, we integrate our framework with existing CKKS bootstrapping techniques and benchmark it against BGV-based implementations of F_{p^r}-arithmetic, a natural baseline for high-throughput finite-field computation. Across the fields we tested, this yields speedups ranging from 1.7x to 178x in amortized multiplication time when bootstrapping is taken into account. The gains are parameter-dependent: roughly speaking, our advantage over BGV increases as the characteristic p becomes smaller and the extension degree r becomes larger.

item.title: Lynx: Symmetric Primitive for Shorter and Faster VOLE-in-the-Head Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1099

AI Summary (中文)

Lynx：面向VOLE-in-the-Head签名的对称原语优化突破

VOLE-in-the-Head（VOLEitH）是构建后量子数字签名最具前景的对称原语驱动框架之一，其安全性根植于乱码电路与向量OLE（VOLE）协议的组合。然而，现有对称原语（如Rainier、AIMer、FAESTv2所用）均未针对VOLEitH的结构性需求进行定制——尤其未优化VOLE相关性数量这一决定签名效率的核心瓶颈。本文提出首个VOLEitH原生友好的对称原语——Lynx，在理论与实践层面实现双重最优：其VOLE相关性需求达到信息论下界，直接压缩签名尺寸并加速计算。

Lynx采用创新的多分支结构，核心包含两项关键设计：（1）定制化非线性层：引入新型截断函数，同步最小化见证长度、多项式度数及有限域乘法次数；（2）策略性线性层交织：在非线性组件间动态插入轻量线性变换，在几乎零开销前提下显著增强抗差分/代数攻击能力。我们通过系统性安全分析，覆盖经典与量子敌手模型下的所有已知攻击路径（含密钥恢复、伪造、相关密钥分析），严格证明Lynx满足VOLEitH框架所需的强不可区分性与抗碰撞性。

基于Lynx，我们构建签名方案Lynxer，在VOLEitH框架下首次实现“更短+更快”的统一突破。实验表明：相较于同设定下最先进方案（Rainier’22、AIMer’23、FAESTv2’25），Lynxer将公钥+签名总尺寸降低25%–51%；签名耗时最高提升90.6%，验证耗时最高提升89.3%，且全部性能优势在标准x86平台经OpenSSL优化实现验证。Lynx不仅填补了VOLEitH专用对称原语的空白，更重新定义了对称密码学在后量子签名中的效率边界。

AI Summary (English)

VOLE-in-the-Head (VOLEitH) is a leading symmetric-primitive-based framework for post-quantum signatures, but existing primitives are not tailored to its structural constraints—especially the number of required VOLE correlations, which critically govern signature size and speed. We introduce Lynx, the first VOLEitH-native symmetric primitive, provably minimizing VOLE correlations to the information-theoretic optimum. Lynx features a multi-branch design with a novel truncation function: nonlinear components are optimized to reduce witness length, polynomial degree, and finite-field multiplications; linear layers are strategically interleaved to strengthen security without overhead. Its security is rigorously analyzed against all classical and quantum attacks in the VOLEitH model. Building on Lynx, we propose Lynxer, a concrete signature scheme that outperforms all prior symmetric-based PQ signatures: it reduces public-key + signature size by 25–51% and improves signing/verification time by up to 90.6%/89.3% versus Rainier (CCS’22), AIMer (CCS’23), and FAESTv2 (Crypto’25).

Abstract

VOLE-in-the-Head (VOLEitH) is one of the most promising frameworks to design post-quantum digital signatures based on symmetric primitives. However, all existing symmetric primitives do not capture the specialized characteristics of the VOLEitH framework and are not VOLEitH-friendly, leaving room for improving the efficiency of VOLEitH-based signatures. In this paper, we propose a VOLEitH-friendly symmetric primitive called Lynx, which is optimal in terms of the number of required VOLE correlations that directly determines the efficiency of VOLEitH-based signature schemes. In particular, Lynx adopts a multi-branch structure featuring a new truncation function: (a) nonlinear components are customized to minimize the witness length and polynomial degree, as well as the number of finite-field multiplications; (b) linear layers are strategically interleaved to strengthen security. The security of Lynx is rigorously validated by covering all possible attacks in the presence of both classical and quantum adversaries. Built upon Lynx, we design a post-quantum signature scheme, Lynxer, in the VOLEitH framework, which is shorter and faster than all known post-quantum signature schemes from symmetric primitives. According to our experimental results, compared to the state-of-the-art symmetric-based signature schemes in the same setting, i.e., Rainier (CCS’22), AIMer (CCS’23) and FAESTv2 (Crypto’25), our signature scheme Lynxer reduces the “public-key size + signature size” by 25% ∼ 51%, and improves the signing (resp., verification) time up to 90.6% (resp., 89.3%).

item.title: Dishonest Majority Multi-Party Arithmetic Garbling with Constant Rate
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1105

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在高延迟网络中部署安全多方计算（MPC）时，最小化通信轮数是核心目标。现有常数轮协议（如基于布尔电路的GMW或BMR变体）虽具实用性，但存在两大瓶颈：（1）每逻辑门带宽开销与安全参数呈线性关系；（2）对算术密集型任务（如隐私保护机器学习、统计分析），将加法/乘法编译为布尔门会引发显著电路膨胀与通信开销。而主流算术协议（如SPDZ）虽高效，却需与电路深度成正比的交互轮数，导致严重延迟。

方法与创新

本文首次提出一种恶意安全、常数轮且常数率的算术MPC协议，适用于$n$方参与、最多$n-1$方被静态恶意敌手控制的场景。关键突破在于：

  • 基于Ball等（Eurocrypt 2023）提出的算术混淆框架，结合BMR模板构建端到端协议；
  • 混淆阶段依赖决策性合数剩余假设（DCR），预处理阶段采用带噪声学习问题（LPN）实现轻量级生成；
  • 定义通信率为“单方带宽 / （门数 × 每门操作数位宽）”，本方案实现该率恒定（即不随规模增长），突破传统线性开销瓶颈；
  • 直接在有界整数域上运算，避免位分解引入的额外开销。

实验结果

在矩阵-向量乘法（数据科学基础算子）基准测试中，相比当前最优常数轮布尔MPC基线（含完整输出位分解开销）：

  • 通信带宽降低101×–247×；
  • 端到端运行时间加速4.4×–10.7×。

该结果验证了协议在理论严谨性与工程实用性上的双重突破，为算术密集型隐私计算提供了首个兼具低延迟与高吞吐的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first concretely efficient, maliciously secure multi-party computation (MPC) protocol that achieves both constant round complexity and constant communication rate, where rate is defined as bandwidth per party divided by gate count and operand size. Built upon Ball et al.’s arithmetic garbling framework (Eurocrypt 2023) and the BMR template, our protocol operates over bounded integers and tolerates up to $n-1$ statically corrupted parties. It relies on the Decisional Composite Residuosity assumption for garbling and Learning Parity with Noise for preprocessing. Evaluated on matrix-vector multiplication, it reduces communication bandwidth by 101×–247× and improves end-to-end runtime by 4.4×–10.7× over state-of-the-art constant-round Boolean MPC baselines—even when accounting for full bit-decomposition of outputs. This bridges a critical gap between low-latency design and arithmetic efficiency in practical MPC.

Abstract

Minimizing round complexity is a central goal in secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC), particularly for deployment on high-latency networks. While constant-round protocols with concrete efficiency have been constructed, they are typically designed for Boolean circuits and each gate incurs a bandwidth cost linear in the security parameter. Moreover, for arithmetic-heavy applications such as privacy-preserving machine learning and statistical analysis, compiling arithmetic operations into Boolean gates incurs another substantial overhead in circuit size and communication. Conversely, existing arithmetic MPC protocols, such as SPDZ, require interaction rounds proportional to the circuit depth, imposing significant latency.

In this work, we bridge this gap by presenting the first concretely-efficient maliciously-secure MPC protocol that achieves both constant-round and constant-rate communication, where the rate is defined as the bandwidth cost per party divided by the number of gates and the size of the values each gate operates on. Our protocol computes over bounded integers and is secure against a static, malicious adversary corrupting up to $n-1$ parties. The protocol is built upon the arithmetic garbling framework of Ball et al. (Eurocrypt 2023) and follows the BMR template, assuming the Decisional Composite Residuosity for the garbling phase and Learning Parity with Noise for preprocessing.

We evaluate our protocol on matrix-vector multiplication, a fundamental operation for data analysis. For standard computation parameters, we reduce communication bandwidth by $101\times$ to $247\times$ and improves end-to-end runtime by $4.4\times$ to $10.7\times$ compared to state-of-the-art constant-round Boolean MPC baselines, even when accounting for the overhead of a full bit-decomposition on the output vector.

item.title: The ABC of Symmetric Primitives over Integer Rings: Milk Before Meat
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1104

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统对称密码设计多基于有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{p^n}^t$（如AES的$\mathbb{F}_{2^8}$），其代数结构清晰、攻击模型成熟。然而，面向同态加密、安全多方计算等新兴场景，整数环 $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$ 上的对称原语日益重要——因其天然支持模幂、加法同态及高效大整数运算。但该领域缺乏系统性理论支撑：已有环上密码（如早期$\mathbb{Z}_{2^n}$分组密码）屡遭快速攻破，根源在于$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$与$\mathbb{F}_{p^n}^t$存在本质差异——前者含零因子、非域、多项式表示不唯一，导致经典差分/线性/代数攻击无法直接迁移。

方法与创新

本文首次构建整数环上对称密码设计的严格基础框架。我们：

  • 系统评估现有统计与代数攻击（如高阶差分、多项式插值）在$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$下的有效性边界；
  • 提出两类新攻击：零因子传播攻击（利用非单位元乘法坍塌特性）与模降阶代数攻击（通过模$p$约化暴露低阶结构）；
  • 关键发现：S盒是否具有多项式表示（即能否写成$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}[x]$中多项式）直接影响安全性——多项式S盒易受代数攻击，而非多项式S盒可天然抵抗此类威胁；
  • 定义两个新度量指标：零因子敏感度（Zero-Divisor Sensitivity）与模降阶鲁棒性（Mod-Reduction Robustness），量化非多项式S盒的抗攻击能力；
  • 给出两种实用构造：1）基于逐位数字操作（digit manipulation）的非多项式S盒（如对$p$进制各位独立置换后重组）；2）基于不可约多项式复合+模截断的混合构造。

意义

本工作 bridging theory and practice：既为环上密码提供首个可验证的安全准则，又产出可直接部署的S盒设计工具，推动隐私计算底层原语的标准化。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first rigorous foundation for designing secure symmetric primitives over integer rings $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$, motivated by applications in homomorphic encryption and MPC. We analyze the failure of standard attacks (e.g., differential, linear, algebraic) in this setting due to structural differences from fields—especially zero divisors and non-unique polynomial representations. We introduce two novel attacks exploiting these features: zero-divisor propagation and mod-reduction algebraic attacks. Crucially, we show that non-polynomial S-boxes—functions not representable as polynomials over $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}$—exhibit inherent resistance; we formalize this via two new security metrics: Zero-Divisor Sensitivity and Mod-Reduction Robustness. Finally, we provide two concrete constructions of such S-boxes, including one based on digit-wise manipulation in base-$p$ representation. Our framework bridges theory and practice, enabling both security proofs and efficient implementations.

Abstract

Designing a secure symmetric-key cipher over a vector space over a field $\mathbb F_{p^n}^t$ is well known and understood by the cryptographic community. Even if the attacks are continuously improving, our current understanding regarding the design and security of the majority of the symmetric-key primitives has not fundamentally changed in the last 20 years.

How does this picture change when we move to an integer ring $\mathbb Z_{p^n}^t$? Although the question is easy to state, it turns out to be far harder to answer. Indeed, there is a significant difference between the arithmetics of $\mathbb F_{p^n}^t$ and $\mathbb Z_{p^n}^t$ and attack vectors do not apply/translate directly between the two. As a case in point, a few ciphers have already been designed over integer rings, yet their initial versions have already been broken.

In this paper, we lay the foundations for a more rigorous approach to designing ciphers over integer rings, noting that this is not only of theoretical interest, but also has concrete applications.
