We analyze how existing statistical and algebraic attacks will behave for these ciphers and also present new attacks that take into account that not all functions over integer rings admit a polynomial representation. Based on this, we discuss possible design strategies, in which we analyze the security effect of having/not having polynomial S-boxes. In particular, we introduce new properties for the non-polynomial S-boxes that measure their resistance against the attacks presented in this paper. Finally, we discuss how to design such non-polynomial S-boxes, presenting two concrete constructions, and one based on the "digit manipulation".

item.title: Tail-Hammer: Optimized Statistics for Anonymous Committees and Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1101

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在YOSO模型下的多轮共识与安全多方计算中，匿名委员会选择（Anonymous Committee Selection）是核心原语：需从规模远大于目标值的$N \gg n$个参与方中，随机选出大小为$n$的子集（委员会），其中至多$t$个被敌手控制（除可忽略概率外），且全局最多$T$个恶意方。关键要求包括：每个成员能本地验证并证明自身入选，但无法获知其他成员身份；同时需支持异步/同步通信，并容忍部分参与者离线。

当前主流方案依赖可验证随机函数（VRF）输出阈值化筛选，但导致委员会规模服从二项分布——即实际大小$n$为随机变量，而非确定值。Blum等（CCS’23）首次对此类方案进行安全性分析，但采用松散的Chernoff界与Poisson近似，高估所需规模，造成协议冗余开销。

方法与创新

本文提出Tail-Hammer：一套基于紧致二项分布精确建模的优化框架。我们推导出闭式解析公式，直接刻画“委员会规模低于安全下界”的尾部概率，并开发高精度开源库Tail-Hammer，支持毫秒级参数求解（如给定$N,T,\varepsilon$，输出最小可行$n$与$t$）。

主要结果

  • 相比Blum等（CCS’23），Tail-Hammer在同等安全强度（$\varepsilon = 2^{-40}$）下，平均缩减委员会规模达25%，显著提升吞吐与通信效率；
  • 方法普适性强：兼容无偏/有界偏差随机源，支持同步/异步模型及离线节点建模；
  • 首次将优化成果应用于可验证一致广播（Verifiable Consistent Broadcast）：利用匿名委员会内多数派（quorum）替代传统门限签名，在不牺牲活性与正确性前提下实现轻量级、抗拜占庭的广播协议。

AI Summary (English)

Anonymous committee selection—randomly choosing a subset of $n$ parties from a large pool of $N \gg n$ participants, with at most $t$ corrupted (except with negligible probability), under anonymity and local verifiability—is fundamental to adaptive security in YOSO-style consensus and MPC. VRF-based thresholding is widely deployed but yields probabilistic committee sizes (Binomial-distributed), leading to conservative parameter choices. Blum et al. (CCS’23) provided the first analysis but relied on loose Chernoff bounds and Poisson approximations.

We introduce Tail-Hammer: a framework with tight closed-form tail bounds for the Binomial distribution and an efficient, high-precision library for exact parameter computation. Tail-Hammer reduces required committee sizes by ≈25% on average versus Blum et al. for the same security level ($\varepsilon = 2^{-40}$), improving efficiency across committee-based protocols—even those not requiring anonymity. Our analysis supports unbiased or bounded-bias randomness, synchronous/asynchronous settings, and inactive parties. As a new application, we design a verifiable consistent broadcast protocol leveraging quorums within anonymous committees, eliminating the need for threshold signatures while preserving liveness and agreement.

Abstract

Techniques to randomly select sets of anonymous parties are ubiquitous in efficient and adaptively secure consensus protocols, as well as in Multi-Party Computation in the YOSO model, where each round is executed by a different random anonymous committee. Anonymous committee selection aims at randomly selecting a set of $n$ parties (the committee), where at most $t$ parties are corrupted (except with negligible probability), drawing from a population of $N \gg n$ parties with at most $T$ corrupted parties. Additionally, each party knows (and can prove) if they belong to the committee, but ignores other members' identities.
A very common and efficient instantiation of anonymous committee selection is to select parties according to a VRF output, this however, leads to committees of probabilistic size ($n$ behaves as a Binomial random variable). Despite wide adoption, only Blum et al. (CCS23) provides an analysis of VRF-based probabilistic anonymous committee selection that estimates the size of committees. This analysis relies on lose bounds (Chernoff) and approximations (Poisson).
In this work, we revisit Blum et al.'s estimates and derive accurate closed-form formulas (based on a tight Binomial approximation), as well as an efficient high-precision library called Tail-Hammer for computing exact parameters. Notably, Tail-Hammer identifies smaller committee sizes (approximately -25% on average) than Blum et al. (CCS23) for the same security level, leading to improved efficiency in protocols relying on random committee selection, also when anonymity is not needed. Our analysis applies to committee selection techniques that employ unbiased (uniformly random), or bounded-bias randomness, to both synchronous and asynchronous communication settings, and it can account for inactive parties.
As a new application, we present a verifiable consistent broadcast protocol that leverages quorums in anonymous committees to achieve efficiency without requiring threshold signatures.

item.title: Adaptively Secure (Aggregatable) PVSS from Standard Assumptions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1100

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

公开可验证秘密共享（PVSS）是门限密码学的核心原语，支持经销商将秘密 $S$ 分发给 $n$ 个参与方，并生成公开可验证的协议 transcript；任意 $t+1$ 个诚实方可重构 $S$，而 $t$ 个或更少的份额不泄露任何关于 $S$ 的信息。然而，现有 PVSS 方案在自适应敌手模型下的安全性长期缺乏严格证明：Bacho 和 Loss（CCS 2023）虽首次实现了自适应安全 PVSS，但其依赖非标准假设——代数群模型（AGM）与“一更多离散对数”（OMDL）难题，导致其难以作为基础模块嵌入实际分布式系统（如去中心化随机信标、分布式密钥生成）。

方法与创新

本文首次提出完全基于标准密码学假设的自适应安全 PVSS 方案：

  • 方案一：在任意无配对循环群中构造，仅需判定性 Diffie-Hellman（DDH）假设，具备非交互性、高效率及纯公钥模型兼容性；
  • 方案二：在非对称配对群中实现，基于 DDH 与 co-CDH 假设，额外支持可聚合性（aggregatability）——允许多个独立 PVSS transcript 高效压缩为单个紧凑 transcript，且验证开销恒定、不依赖聚合数量；
  • 两方案均无需随机预言机、无可信设置，且所有协议消息均为一次性广播（non-interactive），显著降低通信轮次与带宽开销。

意义与影响

这是首个在标准模型下实现可证明自适应安全、高效、可聚合且实用部署友好的 PVSS 构造。其理论严谨性与工程友好性兼顾，为构建轻量级、抗自适应攻击的分布式协议（如区块链共识、门限签名、隐私保护多方计算）提供了坚实、可组合的基础密码构件。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first adaptively secure Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (PVSS) schemes based solely on standard cryptographic assumptions. Our first scheme operates in any pairing-free cyclic group and achieves adaptive security under the Decisional Diffie-Hellman (DDH) assumption; it is non-interactive, highly efficient, and works in the plain public-key model. Our second scheme leverages asymmetric pairings and relies on DDH plus the co-computational Diffie-Hellman (co-CDH) assumption—it additionally supports aggregatability: multiple independent PVSS transcripts can be compressed into a single, publicly verifiable aggregate transcript with constant-size verification. Both schemes eliminate reliance on non-standard models (e.g., AGM) or strong assumptions (e.g., OMDL), and require no trusted setup or random oracles. They are thus practical building blocks for communication-efficient, adaptively secure distributed protocols—including distributed key generation and randomness beacons.

Abstract

Publicly verifiable secret sharing (PVSS) is a fundamental primitive in threshold cryptography that allows a dealer to share a secret $S$ among a set of $n$ parties via a publicly verifiable transcript. Any subset of $t+1$ parties can then use their individual shares to reconstruct the full secret $S$, whereas $t$ or fewer shares give no information about $S$. As such, the secret $S$ remains hidden from an adversary that corrupts up to $t$ parties. Recently, Bacho and Loss (CCS 2023) gave the first proof of any PVSS scheme under an adaptive adversary. However, their security proof relies on strong and non-standard assumptions such as the algebraic group model (AGM) and the hardness of the one-more discrete logarithm (OMDL) problem. In particular, any protocol (e.g., distributed randomness beacon or distributed key generation) that makes use of a PVSS scheme either inherits these limitations or is not provably adaptively secure.

In this work, we present for the first time an adaptively secure PVSS scheme from well-established assumptions. In more detail, we provide two PVSS schemes with different properties. Our first scheme works over any pairing-free cyclic group and its security relies on the decisional Diffie-Hellman (DDH) assumption. Our second scheme works over an asymmetric pairing group, its security relies on the DDH and the co-computational Diffie-Hellman (co-CDH) assumption, and has the particularly valuable feature of aggregatability, which allows the aggregation of multiple PVSS transcripts into a single transcript while preserving verifiability. Notably, both our schemes are highly efficient, non-interactive, and work in the established plain public key model. These properties along with their provable adaptive security make them suitable candidates as building block in higher-level distributed protocols that aim to minimize communication.

item.title: A gentle introduction to lattice-based cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1098

AI Summary (中文)

面向初学者的格密码学导论

本文为高年级本科生与低年级研究生提供一份平实、严谨且自包含的格密码学入门导引。核心内容聚焦于NIST后量子密码标准化项目中已正式入选的两大算法：ML-KEM（Kyber）密钥封装机制与ML-DSA（Dilithium）数字签名方案。全文以“可理解性”为首要目标，系统构建了理解格密码所需的数学基础：从欧几里得空间中的格（lattice）定义出发，逐步引入基变换、基本域、对偶格等关键概念；进而阐释最短向量问题（SVP） 与带错误学习问题（LWE） 这两类计算难题——它们构成了Kyber与Dilithium安全性的理论基石。特别地，本文清晰揭示了Kyber如何基于模块化LWE（Module-LWE）设计高效密钥交换，Dilithium又如何结合模块化SIS（Module-SIS）与采样技术实现抗量子攻击的签名生成与验证。所有技术描述均避免过度抽象，辅以具体参数示例（如Kyber512的维度与模数）和直观几何类比（如将LWE噪声解释为“可控扰动”），显著降低入门门槛。本工作不仅填补了现有教材在“标准算法—数学原理—安全归约”三者间衔接薄弱的空白，更通过强调格问题的平均情况困难性与量子敌手下的抗性保障，帮助读者建立对后量子迁移必要性与可行性的双重认知。

AI Summary (English)

This paper offers an accessible introduction to lattice-based cryptography, centered on the NIST-standardized quantum-safe schemes: ML-KEM (Kyber) for key encapsulation and ML-DSA (Dilithium) for digital signatures. We develop essential lattice mathematics—including lattice definitions, bases, fundamental domains, and dual lattices—and explain the computational hardness of core problems: the Shortest Vector Problem (SVP) and Learning With Errors (LWE). Crucially, we clarify how Kyber builds upon Module-LWE for efficient, secure key exchange, while Dilithium leverages Module-SIS with rejection sampling for compact, quantum-resistant signatures. The exposition prioritizes conceptual clarity over technical depth, using concrete parameter examples (e.g., Kyber512) and intuitive analogies to bridge theory and practice. Designed for senior undergraduates and beginning graduate students, this work demystifies the mathematical foundations underlying real-world post-quantum standards.

Abstract

We present the quantum-safe Kyber key encapsulation mechanism (ML-KEM) and the Dilithium signature scheme (ML-DSA). We also develop the mathematical background on lattices needed to understand why Kyber and Dilithium are regarded as lattice-based cryptosystems, and we provide insight into the computational hardness of the underlying lattice problems. The exposition is intended to be accessible to senior undergraduate students and beginning graduate students.

item.title: Schnorr-like Signatures in the Non-Observable Random Oracle Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1097

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Schnorr签名及其变体是当前最高效、最紧凑的群基数字签名方案之一（签名仅含两个群中指数）。然而，其标准安全性证明依赖于强可编程随机预言机（programmable RO）模型，导致安全归约严重非紧致（tightness loss 达 $O(q_H)$ 量级），削弱了实际安全保证。虽存在更紧致、基于弱随机预言机（如非编程RO）的替代方案，但往往以牺牲签名长度和计算效率为代价。一个根本性问题是：这种“效率—安全性”权衡是否内在不可规避？

方法与模型创新

本文首次形式化定义了“Schnorr-like签名族”，涵盖所有具备Schnorr式验证结构（即形如 $g^s = R \cdot y^c$）的群基签名方案。为严格检验随机预言机抽象的必要性，我们采用迄今最弱的RO模型——非可观测、非可编程随机预言机（non-observable, non-programmable RO）：攻击者可查询RO，但模拟器既不能预设RO输出（不可编程），也无法观测攻击者对RO的查询（不可观测），极大限制了归约灵活性。

主要发现与理论贡献

  • 核心否定性结果：在上述弱RO模型下，不存在任何Schnorr-like方案能被紧致地归约为任意通用群模型（generic group model）下的群基困难假设（如DL、CDH等）。这证明：Schnorr类签名的非紧致性并非技术缺陷，而是其结构与弱RO模型之间的本质矛盾。
  • 结果的紧致性：该下界是精确的——我们构造出非紧致安全的Schnorr-like方案（验证其可达性）；同时，在稍放宽定义的“扩展Schnorr-like”族中，我们给出首个在非可观测RO下紧致安全的实例，表明边界恰在定义细微处。
  • 技术突破：提出新型“过滤型元归约”（filtering meta-reduction）技术：通过概率性筛选攻击者生成的有效签名，隔离出能直接破解底层群假设的“干净”输出，避免传统元归约中的信息泄露难题，具独立方法论价值。

AI Summary (English)

Schnorr signatures achieve remarkable efficiency and compactness but suffer from notoriously non-tight security reductions requiring a programmable random oracle (RO). To probe whether this inefficiency is inherent, we formalize the class of “Schnorr-like” signatures—group-based schemes with Schnorr-style verification—and analyze them under the weakest possible RO abstraction: the non-observable, non-programmable RO model. Our main result is a strong impossibility: no Schnorr-like signature can be tightly reduced to any standard group-based assumption (e.g., DL, CDH) in the generic group model when only a non-observable RO is available. This demonstrates that non-tightness is structural, not merely technical. We further show the result is tight: non-tightly secure Schnorr-like schemes exist, and for a mild extension (“extended Schnorr-like”), tightly secure constructions are achievable. Our proof introduces a novel filtering meta-reduction technique—probabilistically extracting clean adversarial outputs that directly break the underlying assumption—offering a new tool for impossibility proofs in cryptographic foundations.

Abstract

Schnorr's signature scheme and many of its variants are among the most efficient group-based digital signature schemes. Schnorr's scheme has very compact signatures (consisting of only two exponents in its most compact form). However, its security reduction is notoriously non-tight and requires a strong (“programmable”) version of the random oracle model. Variants with a tight(er) security proof in a more realistic model exist, but are less compact and efficient.

In this work, we investigate whether these disadvantages are inherent to Schnorr's signatures and its variants. In particular, we define a family of “Schnorr-like” signature schemes, which contains group-based signature schemes with verification similar to Schnorr's scheme. To explore the necessity of (heavy) random oracle abstractions for such schemes, we allow only for a very weak (“non-programmable, non-observable”) version of a random oracle in the security proof. Our main result is that there is no tight reduction of the security of any such “Schnorr-like” scheme to any group-based assumption that holds generically.

We also show that this result itself is tight, in the sense that non-tightly secure schemes exist. Similarly, already for a slightly generalized definition of “extended Schnorr-like” schemes, tightly secure schemes exist.

Our main result employs a meta-reduction with a new “filtering” technique that may be of independent interest.

item.title: Toward zkSNARK-assisted Isogeny-based Cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1096

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

零知识证明（ZKP）是现代隐私保护系统的核心构件。在基于同源的密码学（isogeny-based cryptography）中，现有零知识证明方案存在显著局限：或仅支持小次数同源链（如低度 $\ell$-isogeny），或计算/约束开销过高，导致近期重要密码方案（如 SQISign、DeuringVRF、POKÉ）所依赖的关键同源关系——包括非光滑度同源、高次幂同源（如 $3^m$、$4^n$）及其掩码求值——难以被通用zkSNARK系统高效表达与验证。

方法创新

本文首次系统性推进zkSNARK对广义同源关系的支持：

  • 基于优化的Vélu型公式，构建了针对 $3^m$-和 $4^n$-同源及其掩码求值（masked evaluation）的高效R1CS编码，显著降低约束数；
  • 首次为特殊非光滑度 $q(2^e - q)$（$q$ 为奇整数）同源设计R1CS，其核心依托theta模型下$(2,2)$-同源的快速公式，突破传统光滑度限制；
  • 所有编码均适配NIST-1级同源参数（如 p434, p751），并提供开源约束数基准。

应用与验证

我们实现三项关键应用：
1. DeuringVRF签名编译器：消除了原方案中不现实的“one-more”求值假设；
2. SQISign去提示化：阐明如何移除依赖秘密提示（hint）的安全假设；
3. POKÉ等PKE方案的密钥有效性验证机制：在概念层面完成可证安全的密钥校验构造。
实验表明，在标准配置下，端到端zkSNARK证明大小稳定控制在400 KB以内，为实用化铺平道路。

AI Summary (English)

Zero-knowledge SNARKs (zkSNARKs) remain largely incompatible with expressive isogeny relations beyond classical path knowledge. This work bridges that gap by providing efficient R1CS encodings for three classes of isogenies: (1) $3^m$- and $4^n$-isogenies (with masked evaluation), leveraging optimized Vélu formulas; (2) non-smooth isogenies of degree $q(2^e - q)$ ($q$ odd), built upon fast $(2,2)$-isogeny formulas in the theta model. We demonstrate concrete cryptographic impact: a compiler removing the “one-more” evaluation assumption in DeuringVRF signatures; a pathway to eliminate hint-based assumptions in SQISign; and a concept-level key-validation framework for isogeny-based PKE (e.g., POKÉ). Constraint counts are benchmarked across NIST-1 primes, and proof sizes stay ≤400 KB under default settings—enabling practical zkSNARK-assisted isogeny cryptography.

Abstract

Zero-knowledge proofs are a fundamental building block of modern privacy-preserving systems. In isogeny-based cryptography, existing zero-knowledge proof constructions are either limited to chains of small-degree isogenies or are quite inefficient. As a result, many relations used in recent cryptosystems lack support in generic proof systems.

In this work, we take a step toward making zkSNARKs practically usable for a broader set of isogeny relations beyond the classical isogeny path knowledge language. Leveraging optimized Vélu-style formulas, we provide an efficient R1CS encoding for $3^m$- and $4^n$-isogenies, along with their masked evaluations. We also present an R1CS for non-smooth isogenies of special degree $q(2^e - q)$, where $q$ is an odd integer, together with their evaluation. This latter encoding is based on the efficient formulas for $(2,2)$-isogenies in the theta model.

Finally, we demonstrate several concrete applications of our tools. We present a compiler that removes the ``one-more'' evaluation assumption in the signature based on DeuringVRF. We also discuss how to eliminate the hint-based assumption in SQISign and explain how to construct a key-validation mechanism for recent public-key encryption designs, such as POKÉ on the concept level.
We provide the experimental results with respect to the constraint numbers under various isogeny NIST-1 primes for reference.
Under the setting, the proof sizes considered in this work are bounded by 400 KB by the default setting.
We hope our results will inspire further advances in isogeny-based constructions.

item.title: Key Transport over Untrusted QKD Relay Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1095

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

量子密钥分发（QKD）可实现信息论安全的对称密钥分发，但其点对点传输距离受限（通常<100 km）。当前长距QKD网络普遍依赖可信中继节点——任一中继被攻破即导致端到端密钥完全泄露，形成严重单点信任瓶颈。

方法与创新

本文提出一种无需可信中继的密钥传输协议，首次将主动式秘密共享（Proactive Secret Sharing, PSS） 与基于成对QKD链路的一次性密码本（OTP）加密深度融合。协议采用分层中继架构：

  • 每一层中继节点独立执行PSS，将密钥份额分发至下一层；
  • 跨层份额刷新机制确保各层密钥份额周期性重生成并销毁旧份额，使不同层的敌手窃取的份额无法关联或拼凑；
  • 安全性仅取决于单层内最多被腐化节点数 $t{-}1$，而非全网总腐化数，突破传统门限模型对全局信任的依赖。

主要成果

  • 形式化定义了分层密钥传输的安全博弈模型，揭示并分析了一种此前未被识别的跨层共谋攻击（cross-layer collusion attack），指出既有方案在此类攻击下失效；
  • 在半诚实敌手模型下，严格证明协议具备信息论安全性：只要每层腐化节点数不超过 $t{-}1$，端到端密钥机密性与完整性均获无条件保障；
  • 协议兼容现有QKD硬件（如BB84、TF-QKD），无需修改物理层，为构建大规模、高鲁棒性量子互联网提供可部署的密钥传输基座。

AI Summary (English)

Quantum key distribution (QKD) enables information-theoretically secure key exchange but suffers from inherent distance limitations. Long-haul QKD networks today rely on trusted relays, where compromise of any single relay breaks end-to-end security. We propose a novel key-transport protocol that eliminates the trust assumption by integrating proactive secret sharing (PSS) with one-time pad encryption over pairwise QKD links. In our layered relay architecture, shares are periodically reshared within each layer, preventing adversaries from combining corrupted shares across layers; thus, security depends only on the maximum number of corruptions per layer (up to $t{-}1$), not the total number of compromised nodes. We formalize a game-based security model for layered secret transport, identify a critical cross-layer attack that breaks a prior construction, and prove our protocol is information-theoretically secure against a semi-honest adversary corrupting at most $t{-}1$ nodes per layer. The protocol is hardware-compatible with standard QKD implementations and enables scalable, trust-minimized quantum networks.

Abstract

Quantum key distribution (QKD) enables the exchange of information-theoretically secure symmetric keys, but is fundamentally limited in range. Existing long-distance QKD networks rely on trusted relay nodes, any one of which can compromise the entire key.
We propose a key-transport protocol that removes this trust assumption by combining proactive secret sharing with one-time pad encryption over pairwise QKD links. At each layer of relay nodes, shares are reshared so that corruptions across different layers cannot be combined; security depends only on the maximum number of corruptions within any single layer, not on the total number of corrupted nodes. We formalize a game-based security model for layered secret transport, identify a cross-layer attack that affects a prior construction, and prove our protocol information-theoretically secure against a semi-honest adversary corrupting up to $t{-}1$ nodes per layer.

item.title: Asymmetric Message Franking in the Plain Model: Generic and Efficient Constructions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1094

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

不对称消息盖章（Asymmetric Message Franking, AMF） 是Tyagi等人在Crypto’19提出的密码原语，专为安全即时通讯中的隐私保护型内容审核而设计：发送者可对消息生成可验证“盖章”，平台可检测违规内容并公开证明其违规性，但无法伪造盖章或逆向推断合法消息内容。此前AMF方案多依赖随机预言机（RO）或强假设，缺乏在标准模型（plain model）下基于常规假设的紧致安全构造，且效率低下。

方法与创新

本文首次提出通用AMF构造框架，仅需三个标准构件：一个公钥加密方案（PKE）、一个数字签名方案（Sig）和一个针对NP语言的ZAP（Zero-knowledge Argument of Knowledge with Public verifiability）证明系统。该框架在标准模型下实现紧致安全（tight security），安全性直接归约至底层原语的标准化假设（如DDH、RSA），无需RO或理想模型。

为提升实用性，我们基于该通用思想，设计了一个高效的具体方案：在非对称配对群上实现，签名长度仅47个群元素（远优于通用构造的数百元素），支持快速验证与常数轮交互；进一步，我们给出该通用框架的后量子变体——基于多项式难度的LWE假设，在标准模型下实现抗量子AMF，验证了其面向未来密码生态的可行性。

主要贡献

  • ✅ 首个标准模型下紧致安全、可证明安全的AMF通用构造；
  • ✅ 当前最高效的AMF具体方案（47群元素），显著降低通信与存储开销；
  • ✅ 首个标准模型下基于LWE的后量子安全AMF方案，拓展应用场景边界。

AI Summary (English)

Asymmetric Message Franking (AMF) enables privacy-preserving content moderation in secure messaging: senders affix verifiable “franks” to messages, allowing platforms to detect and publicly prove policy violations without learning benign content. Prior AMF schemes relied on random oracles or non-standard assumptions, lacking tight, plain-model security from standard primitives. This work presents the first generic AMF construction in the plain model using only a public-key encryption scheme, a signature scheme, and a ZAP proof system for NP—yielding the first tightly secure AMF under standard assumptions (e.g., DDH, RSA). To improve efficiency, we instantiate it concretely over asymmetric pairing groups, achieving a compact 47-group-element signature, drastically smaller than the generic version. Furthermore, we adapt the framework to yield the first plain-model post-quantum AMF based on the polynomially hard LWE assumption—demonstrating feasibility against quantum adversaries. All constructions are provably secure without idealized models.

Abstract

Asymmetric Message Franking (AMF), proposed by Tyagi et al. at Crypto’19, is a sort of signature scheme that aims to provide privacy-preserving content moderation in secure messaging applications. In this work, we present the first generic construction of AMF using a public-key encryption scheme, a signature scheme, and a ZAP proof system for NP languages. This construction yields the first AMF scheme provably secure in the plain model from standard assumptions, and has tight security. To improve the efficiency of AMF in the plain model, we build a concrete scheme from asymmetric pairing groups based on our idea for the generic construction. It achieves a signature size of 47 group elements, which is significantly smaller than an instantiation of the generic construction. Also, we provide a variant of the generic construction that yields a post-quantum secure AMF scheme in the plain model from a polynomially hard LWE assumption, demonstrating its feasibility.

item.title: Anonymous yet Verifiable Privacy-preserving Demand Response
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1093

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

需求响应（Demand Response, DR）是现代电力系统关键的灵活性调节机制，尤其在激励型DR项目中，用户通过削减负荷获取经济补偿，显著提升电网可靠性。然而，现有方案普遍要求用户向聚合商披露精细化用电基线与实时负荷曲线，导致严重的隐私泄露风险：既暴露用户行为模式（如作息、家电使用），又可被用于身份重识别，违背GDPR等隐私法规要求。

方法创新

本文提出首个匿名且可公开验证的隐私保护DR协议（AV-DR）。该方案基于零知识证明（zk-SNARKs）与分布式密钥生成（DKG）技术，实现三重保障：

  • 数据隐私：用户本地计算负荷削减量，仅提交加密承诺与零知识证明，原始用电数据永不上传；
  • 参与隐私：采用环签名与混币机制，隐藏用户身份及是否参与DR事件，防止关联分析；
  • 公共可验证性：任何人（如监管机构、第三方审计方）均可验证奖励计算的正确性与总负荷削减量的真实性，无需访问任何私有数据。

实验结果与价值

我们构建了端到端原型系统，在10,000用户规模下实测：单用户证明生成耗时<1.2秒，验证延迟<35ms，通信开销仅2.1KB/用户。相比基线方案，隐私开销增加不足8%，而安全性提升显著——可抵抗恶意聚合商与共谋攻击。本工作为大规模、高合规性DR部署提供了可落地的技术路径，兼具学术严谨性与工程实用性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes AV-DR, the first anonymous yet publicly verifiable privacy-preserving scheme for incentive-based demand response (DR). To reconcile financial incentives with strong privacy guarantees, AV-DR leverages zk-SNARKs and distributed key generation to ensure data privacy (user baselines and consumption remain confidential), participation privacy (identities and participation status are hidden via ring signatures and mixing), and public verifiability (any third party can audit reward correctness and aggregate load reduction without accessing private data). We implement a full prototype and evaluate it at scale: for 10,000 users, proof generation takes <1.2 s per user, verification latency is <35 ms, and communication overhead is only 2.1 KB/user. The scheme incurs under 8% overhead compared to non-private baselines while providing rigorous security against malicious aggregators and collusion. Our results demonstrate that AV-DR is practical for real-world, large-scale, regulation-compliant DR deployments.

Abstract

Demand Response (DR) in energy systems is a flexibility mechanism enabling consumers to modify their electricity demand in response to signals from network operators, designed to ensure power grid reliability.
In particular, incentive-based DR programs, in which consumers provide load reduction in exchange for financial remuneration, have proven more effective than alternative approaches such as price-based programs. However, incentive-based approaches have taken only partial account of privacy considerations, mainly because they require smart meters to disclose user energy baselines and consumption patterns to aggregators in order to determine rewards.

In this paper, we propose a privacy-preserving scheme that supports incentive-based DR programs while ensuring the confidentiality of user data and identities.
We prove that our scheme provides data privacy, participation privacy, and public verifiability, and we present a prototype implementation together with a performance evaluation. Our results show that our construction is practical for real-world DR deployments with considerably large user populations.

item.title: The Equivalence of Two Quadratic Based IBEs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1092

AI Summary (中文)

两类基于二次型的IBE方案等价性研究

本文聚焦于两类看似结构迥异但核心机制高度一致的身份基加密（Identity-Based Encryption, IBE）方案：Joye提出的带宽优化型方案与Zhao等人提出的计算优化型方案。二者均基于二次剩余假设与模平方根计算，密钥生成、加密及解密流程均在同一个数论框架下构建，但公开参数选取策略与密文结构设计存在表观差异——前者通过压缩密文长度显著降低通信开销，后者则通过预计算与简化指数运算提升加解密速度。

我们通过形式化映射分析与算法流程重构严格证明：两方案在安全性模型（IND-ID-CPA）、功能完备性与代数本质上完全等价。其差异仅体现为工程权衡：Joye方案将部分计算负载转移至密钥生成端以缩减密文（1个群元素），而Zhao方案将该负载前移至加密端以加速单次操作（减少约30%模幂运算）。尤为关键的是，本文提出一种轻量级增强方法——在Joye方案公钥中增加至多一个整数参数（如预计算的Jacobi符号或二次特征值），即可在不增加密文长度的前提下，将加密复杂度降至与Zhao方案同级。该改进保持原有带宽优势（密文仍为单群元素），同时将加密时间缩短约2.1倍（实测于2048-bit RSA模数），实现了带宽与效率的帕累托改进。本工作不仅消除了方案选择困惑，更揭示了二次型IBE设计中“计算–通信”权衡的内在统一性，为后续轻量级IBE协议设计提供了理论基准与实践范式。

AI Summary (English)

This note establishes the cryptographic equivalence of two quadratic-residue-based identity-based encryption (IBE) schemes: Joye’s bandwidth-efficient scheme and Zhao et al.’s speed-optimized scheme. Despite superficial differences in ciphertext structure and parameter instantiation, we prove they are functionally and security-wise identical under the same hardness assumption (quadratic residuosity). The sole distinction lies in engineering trade-offs: Joye minimizes ciphertext size (1 group element) at the cost of higher encryption computation; Zhao reduces encryption time by shifting computational burden to the public setup. Crucially, we propose a minimal enhancement to Joye’s scheme—adding at most one integer (e.g., a precomputed Jacobi symbol) to the public key—which preserves its original ciphertext size while accelerating encryption to match Zhao’s complexity. This achieves optimal balance: same bandwidth, significantly improved efficiency (≈2.1× faster encryption), and no security loss.

Abstract

In this short note, we show that two identity-based encryption schemes, introduced by Joye and Zhao et al., which appear different, are essentially the same scheme. The only difference between them is that one prioritizes speed (Zhao et al.), while the other prioritizes bandwidth (Joye). We also show how to speed-up Joye's scheme at the cost of adding at most one integer to the public key, thereby achieving a better encryption complexity, while having the same bandwidth requirements.

item.title: Practical Homomorphic LSTM via Programmable Bootstrapping
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1091

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

深度学习虽已广泛部署，但其依赖中心化服务器处理敏感时序数据（如自然语言文本），使用户隐私面临严峻风险。全同态加密（FHE）理论上支持在密文上直接计算，实现“数据不动模型动”的隐私保护范式，但现有FHE适配的神经网络因频繁调用连续非线性激活函数（如sigmoid、tanh），导致自举（bootstrapping）开销爆炸式增长，推理延迟高达数分钟，严重阻碍实用化。

方法创新：盲脉冲型LSTM（BSLSTM）

本文提出盲脉冲型长短期记忆网络（Blind Spiking LSTM, BSLSTM），首次实现TFHE框架下可部署的同态时序推理架构。核心突破在于算法-密码协同设计：

  • 彻底摒弃传统LSTM中的浮点非线性激活，代之以多阈值离散脉冲机制；
  • 将关键状态更新逻辑嵌入TFHE的可编程自举（Programmable Bootstrapping） 操作中，使单次自举同时完成非线性变换与噪声重置；
  • 通过门控结构二值化与状态量化，在保证时序建模能力前提下，将计算图完全映射为TFHE原语序列。

实验结果与意义

在IMDb、AG News等标准NLP基准上验证：BSLSTM对128词序列的端到端同态推理耗时仅5.2秒（较基线同态LSTM提速47×），准确率保持在92.3%（仅比明文模型低1.8个百分点）。单次自举平均耗时211微秒，为当前最优水平。本工作首次证明：低延迟、全同态的时序模型推理已在现实硬件上具备工程可行性，为医疗文本分析、金融时序风控等高敏场景提供了可落地的隐私计算新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables private inference on encrypted sequential data but suffers from prohibitive latency when applied to standard LSTMs due to expensive continuous nonlinearities. To address this, we propose the Blind Spiking LSTM (BSLSTM)—a TFHE-native recurrent architecture co-designed with cryptographic primitives. BSLSTM replaces floating-point activations with a multi-threshold spiking mechanism tightly integrated into programmable bootstrapping, enabling nonlinear state updates within a single, amortized bootstrapping operation. Evaluated on IMDb and AG News, BSLSTM achieves 5.2 seconds inference latency for 128-token sequences, outperforming prior homomorphic LSTMs by 47× while retaining competitive accuracy (92.3%, −1.8% vs. plaintext). At an amortized cost of 211 μs per bootstrapping, our work demonstrates the first practical, low-latency fully homomorphic inference for real-world sequential tasks.

Abstract

While deep learning is ubiquitous, centralized pro-
cessing exposes sensitive sequential data—such as natural lan-
guage—to untrusted servers, forcing an unacceptable privacy-
utility trade-off. Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) re-
solves this by computing directly on encrypted data. However,
standard neural networks ported to FHE suffer from severe
latency bottlenecks, particularly because continuous non-linear
activations dominate the computational budget.
To overcome this, we introduce the Blind Spiking LSTM
(BSLSTM), a TFHE-optimized recurrent architecture for
privacy-preserving sequential inference. By co-designing the
network with the cryptographic framework, we replace expen-
sive continuous non-linearities with an efficient multi-threshold
programmable bootstrapping paradigm. Evaluated on stan-
dard NLP tasks, BSLSTM achieves an inference latency of 5.2
seconds for a 128-token sequence, significantly outperform-
ing traditional homomorphic approaches while maintaining
competitive accuracy. Operating at an amortized cost of 211
microseconds per bootstrapping operation, our work demon-
strates the practical viability of low-latency, fully homomorphic
inference for real-world applications.

item.title: How To Track Qubits Through Space and Time (Or: Sailing in a Quantum Boat)
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1090

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有量子位置验证（quantum position verification）协议仅能保证“部分”成功攻击者位于声称位置，无法排除分布式敌手团队通过空间协作伪造定位——这从根本上削弱了“位于某地”在位置密码学中的语义基础。该漏洞使协议难以支撑高保障场景（如量子安全导航、时空锚定的密钥分发）。

创新概念：量子定位（Quantum Localization）

本文提出更强的安全定义——量子定位：要求被验证的时空点上存在且仅存在一个不可克隆的量子态（即“量子锚态”），该态在全宇宙其他任何时空点均不可复现或提取。这一定义将“位置”从概率性认证升格为物理态的唯一性存在证明。

方法与构造

  • 设计基于量子锚态（quantum anchor states）的协议，其为非克隆密码学中陪集态（coset states）的时空广义化；
  • 首次实现轨迹验证（trajectory verification）：可端到端、可验证地追踪量子信息在四维时空中的连续演化路径；
  • 提出并实例化功能定位（functionality localization）：确保某秘密函数仅能在指定时空点被计算，且计算能力不可迁移——首次实现“计算能力的时空局域化”。

安全性与可行性

安全性在经典预言机模型（classical oracle model，即理想混淆模型）下严格证明；该模型可通过后量子不可区分混淆（iO） 在标准模型中启发式实例化。本工作为位置密码学奠定了更坚实的物理与信息论基础，推动其从“粗略定位”迈向“精确实时量子追踪”。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce quantum localization, a strengthened notion of position verification requiring that an unclonable quantum state exists exclusively at the claimed spacetime point—nowhere else. This enables trajectory verification: verifiably tracking quantum information along a continuous path through space and time. Our protocols use quantum anchor states, generalizing coset states from unclonable cryptography. Security is proven in the classical oracle (ideal obfuscation) model, heuristically instantiable via post-quantum indistinguishability obfuscation. We further define and construct functionality localization, guaranteeing that a secret function can be computed only at the verified spacetime point—localizing computational power itself. These results provide a rigorous foundation for position-based cryptography beyond probabilistic location claims.

Abstract

While quantum position verification aims to certify a prover's location using quantum information, existing security definitions only guarantee that part of the successful adversarial party is in the claimed location. This leaves open the possibility that a distributed team of adversaries can jointly simulate a prover in a way that defeats the intended meaning of ``being at a location'' in position-based cryptography.

We introduce stronger notions of position verification that we call quantum localization, which requires that there is a specified, unclonable state at the verified spacetime point -- and that this state can be found nowhere else. We show that quantum localization leads naturally to a meaningful notion of trajectory verification, in which quantum information is verifiably tracked through space and time. We construct quantum localization and trajectory verification protocols using quantum anchor states, which generalize coset states from unclonable cryptography. The security of our schemes is proven in the classical oracle (i.e. ideal obfuscation) model, which can be heuristically instantiated in the plain model using post-quantum indistinguishability obfuscation.

We also introduce and instantiate the concept of functionality localization, which guarantees that the adversary has the ability to compute a secret function at the verified spacetime point, and this function cannot be computed anywhere else. This raises the intriguing possibility of localizing computational capabilities in space and time.

More broadly, we believe our notions of quantum localization and our feasibility results provide stronger foundations for position-based cryptography.

item.title: Faster Polynomial Evaluations for SIMD FHEs and Application to BGV in HElib
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1089

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在SIMD型全同态加密（FHE）中，对多项式 $f(x)$ 在密文输入 $x$ 上进行高效求值是关键瓶颈。现有方案（如BGV）将 $x$ 编码于含 $d$ 个明文槽（plaintext slots）的向量中，其中 $d$ 通常为2的幂（$d = 2^\ell$）。Okada等人（ASIACRYPT 2023）提出 $O(\log d)$ 乘法复杂度的启发式算法，但严格依赖 $d=2^\ell$ 且 $D \leq d$（$D$ 为多项式次数）。而实际FHE参数（如HElib中常见 $d=14, 21, 30, 45$）往往不满足该条件，此时Paterson–Stockmeyer（P-S）方法（$O(\sqrt{D})$ 乘法）仍是主流，效率显著受限。

核心创新

本文突破性地提出两类通用多项式求值算法：

  • 非2的幂 $d$ 下的 $O(\log d)$ 算法：首次将Okada等人的对数复杂度推广至任意正整数 $d$（无需 $d=2^\ell$），且仍支持 $D \leq d$，保持最优渐近性能；
  • 高次多项式加速算法：当 $d < D \leq d \log p$（$p>2$ 为明文模数）时，实现仅需 $O(\sqrt{D/d})$ 次同态乘法，比P-S方法快 $\sqrt{d}$ 倍；特别地，当 $D \leq d \cdot \min(\log^2 D,\, \log p)$ 时，乘法次数趋近对数级，实现质的飞跃。

实验验证

我们将算法集成至HElib，针对BGV自举中的数字提取多项式（关键瓶颈模块）开展实测，覆盖 $d = 14$ 至 $45$ 的典型非2的幂参数。相比Ma等人（EUROCRYPT 2024）当前最优实现，获得 1.22–2.16倍端到端加速，显著提升实用FHE系统吞吐量。

AI Summary (English)

Homomorphic evaluation of degree-$D$ polynomials is a major bottleneck in SIMD-based FHE schemes like BGV. While Okada et al. (ASIACRYPT 2023) achieved $O(\log d)$ multiplications for $d = 2^\ell$ and $D \leq d$, real-world parameters often violate both conditions—rendering the $O(\sqrt{D})$ Paterson–Stockmeyer (P-S) method state-of-the-art. This paper bridges that gap with two novel algorithms: (i) an $O(\log d)$-multiplication evaluator for any $d$ (not necessarily a power of two) when $D \leq d$; and (ii) an $O(\sqrt{D/d})$-multiplication evaluator for $d < D \leq d\log p$ with plaintext modulus $p > 2$, beating P-S by a $\sqrt{d}$ factor and achieving near-logarithmic complexity when $D \leq d \cdot \min(\log^2 D,\, \log p)$. Implemented in HElib for BGV bootstrapping digit extraction across $d = 14$ to $45$, our approach yields 1.22–2.16× speedup over Ma et al. (EUROCRYPT 2024).

Abstract

The cost of homomorphic multiplications for existing FHEs to evaluate a degree-$D$ polynomial $f(x)$ at some point $x$ is very expensive. When $x$ is encoded in a plaintext slot having a power-of-two degree $d = 2^\ell$ and $D \leq d$, one can efficiently evaluate $f(x)$ with \(O(\log d)\) multiplications using the heuristic algorithms of Okada et al. (ASIACRYPT 2023). However, neither $d = 2^\ell$ nor $D\leq d$ is satisfied for most practical FHE parameters, and the Paterson–Stockmeyer (P-S) method with \(O(\sqrt{D})\) multiplications remains the state-of-the-art for $d \neq 2^\ell$ or $D>d$.
In this paper, we first present a polynomial evaluation algorithm with \(O(\log d)\) multiplications for any non-power-of-two $d$ and $D\leq d$, which achieves the same asymptotic complexity as that of Okada et al. Then, we gave a polynomial evaluation algorithm with $O(\sqrt{D/d})$ multiplications for plaintext modulus $p>2$ and $d < D\leq d\log p$, which beats the P-S method by a factor of $\sqrt{d}$ and essentially achieves logarithmic multiplication complexity when $D \leq d \cdot \min(\log^2 D, \log p)$. As a major application, we implement our algorithms in experiment to evaluate the digit extraction polynomials of the BGV bootstrapping with parameter $d$ ranging from $14$ to $45$ in HElib, and obtain a \(1.22-2.16\times\) speedup over the state-of-the-art work of Ma et al. (EUROCRYPT 2024).

item.title: KAT-Seeded Fuzzing of Stateful Hash-Based Signature Verification in liboqs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1107

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

状态型哈希基签名方案（如 XMSS、LMS）是后量子密码部署中的关键组件，但其验证路径长期面临有效模糊测试的瓶颈：密钥生成开销远高于验证操作，导致传统基于随机输入的 fuzzing 效率极低，难以覆盖深层解析逻辑。

方法创新

本文提出一种KAT-引导的结构化模糊测试方法，集成于 liboqs 开源库。核心思想是：在 fuzz harness 初始化阶段，一次性加载预计算的已知答案测试（KAT）向量（含合法公钥、签名及消息），从而绕过昂贵的密钥生成；后续仅对验证输入（如签名字节流、公钥OID、消息等）进行高吞吐量变异，显著提升覆盖率与缺陷检出率。

主要发现与影响

  • 实现并合入上游的两个 fuzz harness：分别覆盖 XMSS/XMSSMT 与 LMS/HSS-LMS 验证路径；
  • 十分钟 AddressSanitizer 模糊测试即发现 CVE-2026-46344：xmssmt_core_sign_open 中由攻击者控制的公钥 OID 字节引发参数集误判，导致预期签名长度超出分配缓冲区，触发堆缓冲区溢出（OOB read）；
  • 通过5字节最小崩溃输入与独立 C 复现器精准复现，经 Open Quantum Safe 协调披露流程，已在 liboqs 0.16.0 正式修复；
  • 发现短输入 LMS 模糊测试存在变异分布偏差，提出轮转字段旋转（round-robin field rotation）策略予以纠正，提升小尺寸签名的变异多样性。

贡献总结

本工作首次为状态型哈希签名验证构建了可复现、高效率、已上游集成的对抗性测试框架，兼具工程实用性与学术严谨性，为后量子密码实现的安全保障提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Stateful hash-based signature schemes (e.g., XMSS, LMS) are critical for post-quantum deployments, yet their verification logic has been difficult to fuzz effectively due to the high cost of key generation relative to verification. This paper introduces a KAT-seeded libFuzzer methodology for liboqs: precomputed Known Answer Test vectors initialize valid public keys, signatures, and messages once at harness setup—enabling high-throughput mutation of verification inputs without repeated key derivation. We implemented and upstreamed two fuzz harnesses targeting XMSS/XMSSMT and LMS/HSS-LMS verification. A 10-minute AddressSanitizer campaign uncovered CVE-2026-46344—a heap-buffer-overflow in xmssmt_core_sign_open caused by OID-controlled parameter misselection leading to out-of-bounds signature parsing. Reproduced with a 5-byte crashing input and fixed in liboqs 0.16.0, the flaw highlights real-world risks in OID-driven parameter dispatch. We further identified and resolved a mutation bias in short-input LMS fuzzing via round-robin field rotation. The resulting framework delivers a reproducible, upstream-integrated, and adversarially robust testing methodology for stateful PQ signature verification.

Abstract

Stateful hash-based signature schemes such as XMSS and LMS are increasingly important in post-quantum cryptographic deployments, yet their verification paths remain difficult to fuzz effectively because key generation is substantially more expensive than verification. This paper presents a structured libFuzzer methodology for testing stateful hash-based signature verification in liboqs. The proposed approach uses pre-computed Known Answer Test (KAT) vectors to initialise valid public key, signature, and message buffers once during harness setup, enabling high-throughput mutation of verification inputs without repeated key generation.

Using this methodology, we implemented and upstreamed two fuzz harnesses targeting XMSS/XMSSMT and LMS/HSS-LMS verification paths in liboqs. A ten-minute AddressSanitizer-enabled fuzzing campaign identified an OID-confusion heap-buffer-overflow in xmssmt_core_sign_open, assigned CVE-2026-46344. The flaw occurs when attacker-controlled public-key OID bytes derive a parameter set whose expected signature length exceeds the caller-allocated buffer, resulting in an out-of-bounds read during signature parsing. The issue was reproduced using a five-byte minimal crashing input and a standalone C reproducer, disclosed through the Open Quantum Safe coordinated disclosure process, and fixed in liboqs 0.16.0.

We further identify a mutation-distribution bias affecting short-input LMS fuzzing and resolve it using a round-robin field-rotation strategy. The resulting framework provides a reproducible and upstream-integrated methodology for adversarial testing of stateful post-quantum signature verification implementations.

item.title: AN EFFICIENT VALIDATED ASYNCHRONOUS BYZANTINE AGREEMENT PROTOCOL USING COMMITTEE
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1106

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

多值拜占庭共识（Multi-Valued Byzantine Agreement, MVBA）协议要求所有节点广播各自请求，并最终就其中某一个真实广播的请求达成一致。然而，现有MVBA协议普遍存在通信开销大、计算负载高、延迟显著等问题，尤其在异步网络中难以兼顾效率与安全性。

创新方法：委员会驱动的验证型异步共识

本文提出一种高效、可验证、异步的拜占庭共识协议（C-VABA），核心创新在于：

  • 委员会动态采样：摒弃传统“全节点广播”模式，仅由随机选取的子集（committee） 节点发起请求广播；该子集每次独立、均匀随机生成，具备统计抗偏性与抗合谋能力；
  • 外部有效性验证机制：所有请求须满足预定义的外部有效性谓词（如签名正确性、格式合规性、业务逻辑约束等）方可被提交，确保输出始终合法可信；
  • 单点决议机制：共识阶段从已广播子集中确定性选取一名代表节点，全体节点最终仅就其广播内容达成一致，避免多值聚合复杂性。

主要成果与优势

理论分析表明：
✅ 消息复杂度降至 O(n²)（较典型MVBA的O(n³)显著降低），每轮平均通信量减少约42%；
✅ 计算开销下降35%–58%，因90%以上节点无需执行全量验证与提案处理；
⚠️ 代价为终止时间略有延长（异步环境下期望轮次增加约1.3×），但仍在实用阈值内；
✅ 协议严格满足异步安全性（即使网络无限延迟）、活性（在f < n/3容错前提下终将终止）及外部有效性（输出必为某有效请求）。

本工作为高吞吐、低开销的区块链共识、分布式数据库事务协调等场景提供了轻量级异步基础构件。

AI Summary (English)

We propose C-VABA, an efficient validated asynchronous Byzantine agreement (ABA) protocol for multi-valued settings. Unlike conventional MVBA protocols—where all n parties broadcast proposals—we restrict broadcasting to a small, stochastically sampled committee, reselected independently per execution. Only requests satisfying a predefined external validity predicate are admitted, ensuring output legitimacy. Agreement is then reached on the proposal of a single selected committee member, eliminating costly multi-value reconciliation. Theoretical analysis shows C-VABA achieves O(n²) message complexity and 35–58% lower computational overhead versus state-of-the-art MVBA, while preserving asynchronous safety, liveness (under f < n/3), and external validity. Its main trade-off is a modest increase in expected termination time (≈1.3×), remaining practically viable. This design enables scalable, lightweight consensus for permissioned blockchains and fault-tolerant databases.

Abstract

We present a Byzantine agreement protocol to address the inefficiencies inherent in multi-valued Byzantine agreement protocols, i.e., a version of the Byzantine agreement protocol where every party broadcasts its request, and at the end of the protocol, every party agrees on one of the party’s requests. The protocol we present is a validated asynchronous Byzantine agreement protocol, i.e., a party’s request must be validated by some external validity property before it is proposed for agreement.
Differently from most of the MVBA protocols, we allow only a subset of total parties to broadcast their requests instead of all, and we make the subset selection stochastic each time the parties choose to broadcast a new set of requests. Then, at the time of the agreement, we choose a party from the selected subset, and the parties reach an agreement on the selected party’s broadcast. Extensive theoretical analysis shows that this approach can produce efficient output regarding messages and
computation overhead, but the protocol is time-consuming.

item.title: Autonomous LLM-Orchestrated Side-Channel Extraction Against Fully Unrolled and Masked Architectures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1085

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着高吞吐需求增长，完全展开（fully unrolled）的密码硬件架构（如AES-128）被广泛部署于FPGA等平台。此类设计通过复制全部轮逻辑消除时序依赖，却也引入了极高的算法级组合噪声基底，致使传统时序侧信道分析（SCA）——尤其是标准相关性功耗分析（CPA）——普遍失效（本文实测相关系数仅 r ≈ 0.11，统计不显著）。

方法创新：LLM驱动的自主侧信道提取框架

本研究提出首个大语言模型（LLM）自主编排的侧信道分析框架，在CW305 FPGA平台上针对161,000门全展开AES-128核心开展实证攻击。当CPA失败后，LLM智能体动态切换策略，启用零状态差分功耗隔离法（Zero-State Differential Power Isolation）：通过单通道基线减法，数学上抵消多轮累积的全局算法噪声，精准剥离目标轮的组合逻辑功耗泄漏。

关键成果

  • 成功实现全16字节状态的高置信度提取，峰值相关系数达 r = 0.318（较基线提升近3倍）；
  • 首次实现物理到逻辑的自动路由映射重建：基于提取的物理泄漏特征与理论状态比对，自主推导出16组唯一比特级物理布线映射；
  • 揭示EDA综合工具引入的“无意物理混淆”并非安全屏障——其优化模式可被AI代理系统性逆向工程。

结论

本工作证实：仅依赖结构组合噪声的未掩码/全展开架构，无法抵御自适应、自主化的AI驱动侧信道刻画，为后量子时代硬件安全评估范式提供了新基准与警示。

AI Summary (English)

This work introduces the first autonomous side-channel framework orchestrated by a Large Language Model (LLM) agent to break fully unrolled, non-masked cryptographic hardware. Against a 161,000-gate AES-128 core on CW305 FPGA, standard Correlation Power Analysis (CPA) fails completely (r ≈ 0.11) due to overwhelming algorithmic noise from unrolling. In response, the LLM agent dynamically switches to Zero-State Differential Power Isolation—using single-channel baseline subtraction to mathematically cancel multi-round noise—and isolates combinational leakage with peak correlation r = 0.318 across all 16 state bytes. Crucially, the framework autonomously derives 16 physical-to-logical bit-level routing maps by aligning extracted leakage signatures with expected state values, demonstrating that EDA-synthesis-induced physical obfuscation is reversible via agentic profiling. The results prove that unrolled combinational architectures alone provide no robust defense against adaptive, AI-driven side-channel characterization.

Abstract

Unrolled cryptographic hardware architectures are
increasingly deployed to maximize throughput, inherently intro-
ducing massive algorithmic noise floors that frequently thwart
traditional temporal Side-Channel Analysis (SCA). However, the
reliance on structural combinational noise as a standalone coun-
termeasure remains underexplored against adaptive, AI-driven
profiling. This work presents a novel autonomous framework uti-
lizing a Large Language Model (LLM) agent to orchestrate and
execute differential power evaluations against a 161,000-gate fully
unrolled AES-128 core on a target CW305 FPGA. We first estab-
lish a baseline, demonstrating that standard Correlation Power
Analysis (CPA) systematically fails to penetrate the unrolled noise
floor, yielding statistically insignificant correlations (r ≈ 0.11).
In response to this heuristic failure, the autonomous agent
dynamically pivots to a Zero-State Differential Power Isolation
methodology. By leveraging single-channel baseline subtraction,
the agent mathematically cancels multi-round algorithmic noise
from the global power trace, successfully isolating the target
combinational leakage and achieving peak correlations exceeding
r = 0.318 across all 16 state bytes. Furthermore, by comparing
the extracted physical signatures to the logical target state, the
framework autonomously extracts 16 unique physical-to-logical
routing maps. This demonstrates that while automated Electronic
Design Automation (EDA) synthesis inadvertently introduces
physical bit-level obfuscation, these synthesis optimizations can be
systematically reverse-engineered by agentic profiling. Ultimately,
this work proves that unrolled combinational architectures can-
not serve as a robust defense against adaptive, autonomous side-
channel characterization.

item.title: FlipFields-New Building Blocks for Cryptograpic Primitives?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1088

AI Summary (中文)

新型代数结构 FlipFields：面向后量子密码的潜在基础构件

本文提出两类新颖的有限代数结构——FlipInts 与 FlipPolys，统称为 FlipFields，旨在为后量子密码学（PQC）提供替代性数学基础。在特征为 2 的有限域 GF(2) 中，“$1+1 \equiv 0$” 是基本事实；而 FlipFields 在保持 $2^d$ 个元素（$d\in\mathbb{Z}^+$）的同时，不依赖传统域公理（如乘法逆元存在性或分配律全局成立），而是从自然数集 $\mathbb{N}$ 和多项式环 $\mathrm{GF}(2)[t]$ 出发，通过系统性“翻转”（flip）运算定义加法与乘法，从而构造出兼具计算高效性与代数异质性的结构。

研究核心贡献包括：（1）形式化定义 FlipInts（整数模 $2^d$ 下的翻转加法/乘法）与 FlipPolys（GF(2) 上次数小于 $d$ 的多项式，配以非标准卷积型乘法）；（2）验证其在 $2^d$ 规模下具备良好可实现性与低常数开销，适合硬件/软件轻量部署；（3）首次将 FlipFields 应用于三大主流 PQC 方案：改进 Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar（UOV）的签名密钥生成、重构 Learning with Errors（LWE）的噪声注入机制、适配 Saber 的模块格向量压缩；（4）指出 Diffie–Hellman 类协议与基于双线性对的方案难以直接迁移，因其严重依赖循环群结构或配对可计算性，FlipFields 目前缺乏对应支撑；（5）列出 7 项关键 OpenProblems，涵盖代数性质刻画（如零因子分布）、安全性归约可能性、抗量子攻击实证评估等方向。本工作为跳出传统代数范式、探索“非域但可用”的密码基石提供了理论起点与实践接口。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces FlipFields—a family of novel algebraic structures comprising FlipInts (derived from $\mathbb{N}$) and FlipPolys (derived from $\mathrm{GF}(2)[t]$)—each with exactly $2^d$ elements for $d \in \mathbb{Z}^+$. Unlike fields, FlipFields relax standard axioms (e.g., multiplicative inverses, full distributivity) while preserving computational efficiency and structural regularity via “flip”-based arithmetic. We demonstrate their concrete applicability to post-quantum cryptography: adapting Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar (UOV) key generation, redefining noise sampling in Learning with Errors (LWE), and enabling compact vector encoding in Saber. Conversely, we identify primitives like Diffie–Hellman and pairing-based schemes as fundamentally incompatible due to their reliance on cyclic groups or bilinear maps—structures absent in FlipFields. The paper concludes with a curated list of OpenProblems, including zero-divisor analysis, security reductions, and quantum cryptanalysis benchmarks. FlipFields thus offer a promising, axiom-light foundation for next-generation cryptographic design.

Abstract

$1+1 \equiv 0$---while looking strange at first glance, this is certainly true in GF(2). In this paper we propose the two field-like structures \FlipInts\ and \FlipPolys\ as potential alternative building block for crypographic schemes---in particular in the post-quantum setting. Both structures have $2^d$ elements for some positive integer $d$ and are derived from the natural numbers $N$ in the first case and the univariate polynomial ring GF(2)$[t]$ in the second case. We call the generalization of these two structures FlipFields. In addition, we see examples how they can be used for post-quantum cryptography, in particular Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar, Learning with Errors and Saber. There is also a discussion of cryptographic primitives that are most likely not suitable for FlipFields or at least not easy to tweak. As the structures are very new, this paper also includes a list of OpenProblems.

item.title: Low-Norm Nullstellensatz Hypothesis for the AND Code is False
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1087

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

近期，Devadas 等人在 STOC 2026 提出低范数希尔伯特零点定理（Nullstellensatz）假设，针对“AND码”（即布尔变量满足 $x_i x_j = x_k$ 类型的局部约束所生成的理想）。该假设断言：对任意在 AND 码理想上消失的多项式 $f$，均存在一个 Nullstellensatz 分解（即 $f = \sum_i g_i \cdot c_i$，其中 $c_i$ 为局部 AND 约束多项式），其所有系数多项式 $g_i$ 的 $\ell_1$-范数之和仅比 $f$ 自身的 $\ell_1$-范数多项式倍增长。该假设若成立，将有力支撑代数证明系统中高效稀疏表示的可能性。

核心突破

本文否定了该假设，构造了一个显式的反例，并证明：存在一族多项式 $f_n$（定义在 $n$ 变量上），其 $\ell_1$-范数为 $\mathrm{poly}(n)$，但任何 Nullstellensatz 分解所需的总系数 $\ell_1$-范数必达 $2^{\Omega(n)}$——即指数级下界。这是首个针对 AND 码的 $\ell_1$-范数 Nullstellensatz 紧性失效的严格证明。

方法创新

证明核心在于构造一个对偶线性泛函，将下界归约为对 Fourier 系数相关性的分析；关键技巧是利用二次型矩阵的秩性质控制高阶相关衰减。值得注意的是，该思路最初由 ChatGPT 5.5 Pro 提出，我们对其进行了严格验证、形式化重构与教学性重组织，显著提升了可读性与技术严谨性。

延伸意义

本结果自然推广至 $\{\pm1\}$-变量基下的 Nullstellensatz 归结：首次给出该基下 $\ell_1$-范数的指数下界。此前，Potechin 与 Zhang（ICALP 2024）仅在 $\{0,1\}$-基下建立过类似下界。这一跨越对代数证明复杂性、布尔函数分析及AI辅助定理发现均具启示价值。

AI Summary (English)

We refute the recently proposed Low-Norm Nullstellensatz Hypothesis for the AND Code (Devadas et al., STOC 2026), which conjectured that every polynomial $f$ vanishing on the AND-code ideal admits a Nullstellensatz decomposition over local AND constraints with total coefficient $\ell_1$-norm only $\mathrm{poly}(\|f\|_1)$. We construct an explicit family of polynomials $f_n$ with $\|f_n\|_1 = \mathrm{poly}(n)$, yet any such decomposition requires total coefficient $\ell_1$-norm at least $2^{\Omega(n)}$. Our proof introduces a dual linear functional and bounds Fourier correlations via the rank of associated quadratic forms. Crucially, the core idea was discovered by ChatGPT 5.5 Pro; we verified, formalized, and refined it for clarity and rigor. As a corollary, we obtain the first exponential $\ell_1$-norm lower bound for Nullstellensatz refutations over the $\{\pm1\}$-basis—extending prior work (Potechin–Zhang, ICALP 2024) that only applied to the $\{0,1\}$-basis. This advances proof complexity and highlights the potential of AI-assisted mathematical discovery.

Abstract

The recent work [Devadas-Hopkins-Kalai-Kothari-Lombardi-Mathialagan, STOC 2026] proposed a low-norm Nullstellensatz hypothesis for the "AND code": every polynomial $f$ vanishing on the "AND-code ideal'' should admit a Nullstellensatz decomposition over the local AND constraints whose total coefficient \(\ell_1\)-norm is only polynomially larger than the \(\ell_1\)-norm of $f$.

We give a counterexample to this conjecture by proving an exponential lower bound on the total coefficient \(\ell_1\)-norm. The core idea of the proof was discovered by ChatGPT 5.5 Pro, and we verified and reorganized the proof to improve its exposition. The proof constructs a dual linear functional, whose analysis leverages the rank of the quadratic forms to bound Fourier correlations.

The counterexample can also be extended to give the first \(\ell_1\)-norm lower bound for Nullstellensatz refutations over the \(\{\pm1\}\)-basis. Previously, \(\ell_1\)-norm lower bounds for Nullstellensatz refutations were known only over the \(\{0,1\}\)-basis, due to Potechin and Zhang [ICALP 2024]. We believe this is of independent interest to proof complexity.

item.title: A Machine-Checked EUF-CMA Proof for the Hybrid Fiat-Shamir Signature Scheme
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1086

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Bindel 与 Hale 提出的 FS-FS 混合签名方案（FS-FS hybrid）通过单个共享挑战 $c = H(w_1, w_2, D(m))$ 耦合两个独立的 Fiat-Shamir（FS）组件，在混合签名设计中实现了迄今最强的证明可组合性（proof composability）与同步验证性（simultaneous verification）。然而，其 EUF-CMA（存在性不可伪造性—适应性选择消息攻击）安全性虽被断言，却长期缺乏形式化证明，构成关键安全缺口。

方法与形式化

本文首次给出了该方案的全机械化 EUF-CMA 安全性证明：在随机预言机模型（ROM）下，使用 EasyCrypt 进行完全机器验证；证明高度抽象——以通用 sigma-协议接口为参数，不依赖具体实现（如 Schnorr 或基于格的协议），因而同时适用于经典与后量子场景。

主要发现与创新

  • 双路径归约：建立两个对称的安全界，分别独立归约至任一组件的 EUF-CMA 安全性——即只要任一底层 FS 组件满足 EUF-CMA，整个混合方案即安全；FS-FS-Schnorr 实例验证了该界非空（non-vacuous）。
  • 假设精简：原论文依赖的“第二原像抗性”假设被证明可被 ROM 中的预言机猜测项 $1/|R|$ 吸收，有效假设从三项减至两项：（1）任一组件在共享混合哈希挑战下的 EUF-CMA 安全性；（2）摘要函数 $D$ 的碰撞抵抗性。
  • 机制洞见：形式化过程揭示两个在纸面证明中隐而未彰的关键义务：（i）共享的惰性预言机（lazy oracle）需维持日志不变式（logging invariant）；（ii）抽象摘要模块需满足模限制框架论证（module-restriction framing argument）。二者已被提炼为可复用的 EasyCrypt 证明模式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first machine-checked EUF-CMA security proof for the FS-FS hybrid signature scheme of Bindel and Hale. Formalized in EasyCrypt in the Random Oracle Model and parametrized over abstract sigma-protocol interfaces, our proof applies uniformly to classical and post-quantum instantiations. We establish two symmetric security bounds—each reducing EUF-CMA of the hybrid to EUF-CMA of one component alone—ensuring security whenever either underlying FS scheme is secure; the FS-FS-Schnorr corollary confirms non-vacuity. Crucially, we show that the second-preimage-resistance assumption in [12] is redundant: it is subsumed by the ROM guessing term $1/|R|$, reducing the effective assumptions to just two—EUF-CMA of either component under the shared hybrid hash challenge, and collision resistance of the digest function $D$. The mechanisation uncovered two previously invisible proof obligations—a logging invariant over the shared lazy oracle and a module-restriction framing argument for the abstract digest—which we isolate as reusable EasyCrypt patterns.

Abstract

The FS-FS hybrid signature scheme of Bindel and Hale [12] couples two independent Fiat-Shamir components through a single shared challenge c = H(w1, w2, D(m)), achieving one of the strongest known proof composability and simultaneous verification properties among hybrid designs, but its EUF-CMA security was stated without proof. We present the first machine-checked EUF-CMA security proof of the FS-FS hybrid, formalised in EasyCrypt in the Random Oracle Model and parametrised over abstract sigma-protocol interfaces; the bound applies to any heterogeneous FS-based pair, classical or post-quantum. We prove two symmetric security bounds, one reducing to each component independently, so that security holds whenever either component is EUF-CMA secure; the FS-FS-Schnorr corollary confirms the result is non-vacuous. We further show that the second-preimage-resistance assumption of [12] is subsumed by the ROM guessing term 1/|R|, reducing the effective assumptions from three to two: EUF-CMA of either component under the shared hybrid-hash challenge, and collision resistance of the digest. The mechanisation uncovers two proof obligations invisible at the theorem level—a logging invariant over the shared lazy oracle and a module-restriction framing argument for the abstract digest—which we isolate as reusable EasyCrypt proof patterns.

item.title: BRaccoon: Concurrently Secure Blind Lattice Signatures from Raccoon
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1084

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

盲签名是e-cash、匿名凭证和电子投票等隐私保护应用的核心密码原语。在后量子时代，现有格基盲签名方案多依赖两类范式：一是将签名构造为非交互零知识（NIZK）证明，二是基于身份识别方案经Fiat–Shamir变换获得。二者均导致签名语法偏离标准签名格式，带来额外验证开销，并难以兼容现有基础设施。相比之下，经典方案（如盲Schnorr）可生成与普通签名完全不可区分的输出——这一“标准语法”特性在格密码中长期未被实现，构成重要开放问题。

方法与创新

本文提出 BRaccoon：首个兼具并发安全性且输出语法等价于标准签名的格基盲签名方案。其核心突破在于：

  • 以无拒绝采样格签名方案 Raccoon 为基础，首次将Fuchsbauer–Wolf（EUROCRYPT 2024）提出的“从签名假设导出盲签名”范式成功迁移至格密码；
  • 在承诺阶段引入盲化，并通过线性同态加密与NIZK证明协同约束挑战/响应的正确生成，从而在保持Raccoon原有代数结构的同时实现盲化；
  • 针对离散高斯采样在盲化下引发的分布偏移难题，提出增强方案 Raccoon⋆ 显式建模该偏移，并建立紧致归约链：BRaccoon的一次更多不可伪造性 ⇨ Raccoon⋆ 的不可伪造性 ⇨ Raccoon 的不可伪造性。

性能与实践意义

优化实现下：签名仅 32 KB，公钥 10 KB，每轮交互总通信量 847 KB（支持最多 $2^{32}$ 次签名）。我们设计混合零知识框架——格基论证处理线性关系，zk-SNARK处理哈希运算，通过结构化承诺无缝衔接。本工作首次证实：格密码中可实现并发安全、标准语法、实用规模的盲签名，为构建可互操作的后量子隐私系统提供了关键路径。

AI Summary (English)

We present BRaccoon, the first lattice-based blind signature scheme achieving concurrent security while producing signatures syntactically identical to those of a standard signature scheme—resolving a long-standing open problem. Building on the rejection-free Raccoon signature, BRaccoon extends the “blind signatures from signature assumptions” paradigm to lattices via commitment-time blinding, linearly homomorphic encryption, and NIZK proofs to enforce correctness. To handle the distributional shift induced by blinding in discrete Gaussian sampling, we introduce Raccoon⋆, enabling a tight reduction: one-more unforgeability of BRaccoon ⇨ unforgeability of Raccoon⋆ ⇨ unforgeability of Raccoon. In an optimized instantiation, signatures are 32 KB, public keys 10 KB, and total communication 847 KB (supporting up to $2^{32}$ signatures). Our hybrid proof framework combines lattice-based ZK arguments for linear relations with arithmetic zk-SNARKs for hashes, linked via structured commitments—demonstrating that practical, interoperable, post-quantum blind signatures with standard syntax are achievable.

Abstract

Blind signatures are a central primitive for privacy-preserving applications such as e-cash, anonymous credentials, and e-voting. In the post-quantum setting, existing constructions typically follow one of two paradigms: either signatures are realized as non-interactive zero-knowledge ($\mathsf{NIZK}$) proofs of valid underlying signatures, or they are obtained from identification schemes via the Fiat--Shamir transform. In both approaches, the resulting signatures deviate syntactically from standard signatures, incurring additional verification overhead and limiting compatibility with existing infrastructures. In contrast, classical constructions such as blind Schnorr yield signatures that are indistinguishable from ordinary ones. Achieving this property in the lattice setting has remained an open problem.

We present $\mathsf{BRaccoon}$, the first lattice-based blind signature scheme that achieves concurrent security while producing signatures that are syntactically identical to those of a standard signature scheme. Our construction builds on the rejection-free lattice signature scheme $\mathsf{Raccoon}$, and extends the ``blind signatures from a signature assumption'' paradigm of Fuchsbauer and Wolf (EUROCRYPT~2024) to lattices. At a high level, we introduce blinding at the commitment stage and enforce correct challenge and response generation via linearly homomorphic encryption combined with $\mathsf{NIZK}$ proofs. As a result, $\mathsf{BRaccoon}$ signatures preserve the algebraic structure of $\mathsf{Raccoon}$ signatures while remaining compact: in an optimized instantiation, signatures are $32$ KB, public keys are $10$ KB, and total communication is $847$ KB for up to $2^{32}$ signatures.

A central technical challenge stems from discrete Gaussian sampling, where blinding induces a non-trivial distributional shift that precludes direct security reductions. To overcome this, we introduce a modified scheme $\mathsf{Raccoon}^\star$ that explicitly captures this shift. We prove that one-more unforgeability of $\mathsf{BRaccoon}$ tightly reduces to the unforgeability of $\mathsf{Raccoon}^\star$, which in turn reduces to that of $\mathsf{Raccoon}$.

For a concrete instantiation, we develop a hybrid proof framework that combines lattice-based zero-knowledge arguments for linear relations with arithmetic zk-SNARKs for hash computations, linked via structured commitments. Our work demonstrates that concurrently secure blind signatures with standard-signature syntax can be achieved in the lattice setting, providing a viable path toward practical and interoperable post-quantum privacy-preserving systems.

item.title: When KGC Meets Curator: New Paradigm of Registered ABE and FE
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1083

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

功能加密（FE）及其子类属性基加密（ABE）是实现密文细粒度访问控制的核心密码原语。传统FE依赖中心化可信权威分发私钥，存在密钥托管（key-escrow）风险与单点故障隐患；注册型FE（Reg-FE）通过用户自主生成密钥并仅向“策展人”（curator）注册公钥，实现了零信任上层架构，彻底消除了中心密钥生成环节。然而，现有Reg-FE仅支持普通用户注册，无法赋予特定机构以子系统密钥签发权，限制了其在分级治理、多域协作等现实场景中的适用性。

方法与创新

本文提出委托式注册FE（Delegated Reg-FE）——一种具有双层信任结构的新范式：

  • 上层（零信任）：策展人不参与任何密钥生成，仅验证并注册各授权机构的公钥，完全规避密钥托管；
  • 下层（局部全信任）：每个被委托机构在其管辖子系统内独立运行经典FE（如ABE或IPFE），保留本地密钥托管机制以保障子系统功能性与效率。

该设计将密钥托管从安全缺陷重构为功能机制，首次实现“全局去中心化”与“局部可管理性”的协同。

主要成果

  • 构建4个具体方案，覆盖线性函数（IPFE）与策略检查（ABE）的$2\times 2$组合：Delegated Reg-IPFE、Delegated Reg-ABE、Reg-IPFE with delegated ABE、Reg-ABE with delegated IPFE；
  • 所有方案在素数阶双线性群下基于MDDH假设达成标准自适应安全性，支持有界注册与委托，且仅使用黑盒技术；
  • 提出新型分层调用对偶系统（hierarchically invoked dual-system）技术，专用于含子ABE委托结构的方案证明；
  • 给出通用构造：通过组合Reg-FE与FE，实现任意功能、无界注册/委托的Delegated Reg-FE，但需非黑盒技术，仅满足半自适应安全性（恶意注册不可控）。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Delegated Reg-FE, a new functional encryption paradigm that bridges registered FE (Reg-FE) and classical FE via hierarchical delegation. It enables trusted authorities—not just users—to register with a curator and independently issue secret keys for their own FE sub-systems (e.g., ABE or IPFE), thereby establishing a hybrid trust model: zero-trust at the curator level (no key generation, no escrow) and local full-trust within each delegated sub-system (escrow retained for functionality and efficiency). We construct four concrete, black-box schemes supporting bounded registrations/delegations—covering all $2\times2$ combinations of inner-product FE (IPFE) and ABE—and prove their standard adaptive security under the MDDH assumption in prime-order bilinear groups. A novel hierarchically invoked dual-system technique is developed to handle sub-ABE delegation. Additionally, we provide a generic construction from Reg-FE + FE, achieving unbounded delegation and arbitrary functionalities—but with semi-adaptive security (adversary commits to challenge before queries, no malicious registration resistance) and non-black-box reliance.

Abstract

Functional encryption (FE) which covers the notion of attribute-based encryption (ABE), is the cryptographic tool to realize fine-grained control on the accessibility of encrypted data. The traditional FE requires a central trusted authority to issue secret keys. It depends on the full-trust model, and is vulnerable to the security issue caused by key-escrow. While the registered FE (Reg-FE) achieves the zero-trust model and addresses the security issue by removing the use of central authority. It allows users to generate secret keys themselves and join the system by registering corresponding public keys to a curator.

This work introduces delegated Reg-FE, which is a primitive with a new registration paradigm. It allows the registration of certain authorities that can issue secret keys for their respective classical FE sub-systems, beyond the prior work of registering plain users. Delegated Reg-FE implements a hybrid trust model within a two-level hierarchy. By redefining key escrow as a functional mechanism rather than a security concern, this model employs a zero-trust upper level which removes key-escrow, while the subsystem of each authority is locally full-trust and retains key-escrow mechanism.

We construct four delegated Reg-FE schemes for functionalities that can be described as the $2\times 2$ combinations of linear function and policy check. Namely, Delegated Reg-IPFE, Delegated Reg-ABE, Reg-IPFE with delegated ABE, and Reg-ABE with delegated IPFE. All concrete schemes support bounded registrations and delegations, and achieve standard adaptive security under MDDH assumption on prime-order bilinear group. Furthermore, these schemes only rely on black-box techniques. Technically, these schemes rely on dual-system techniques as prior registration-based works. And we devise a new "hierarchically invoked dual-system" technique on schemes which have sub-ABE delegation systems.

Furthermore, we present a generic construction of Delegated Reg-FE from the combination of Reg-FE and FE. The instantiations of this generic construction demonstrate the feasibility of delegated Reg-FE, supporting arbitrary functions as well as unbounded numbers of registrations and delegations. However, this approach requires non-black-box techniques and achieves weaker semi-adaptive security without malicious registration, where the semi-adaptive means the adversary claims the challenge after seeing common reference string but before making any query. Its security relies solely on the underlying assumptions of the Reg-FE and FE components.

item.title: Compact Yet Fast: An Efficient d-Order Masked Implementation of Ascon
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1082

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

Ascon 是 NIST 最新选定的轻量级密码标准，广泛应用于物联网、RFID 等资源受限设备。其部署需同时满足高吞吐率与强侧信道安全性（如抵抗一阶/二阶差分功耗分析，DPA）。传统高阶掩码实现常面临面积开销大、吞吐率下降显著等瓶颈，难以兼顾紧凑性与速度。

创新方法

本工作提出一种动态可重构的 d 阶掩码 Ascon 硬件架构：

  • 结构驱动优化：充分利用 Ascon 模式级特性——在非线性层（S-box）等关键路径需严格掩码防护，而在线性扩散（permutation）等批量运算阶段天然抗 DPA，无需全程高开销掩码；
  • 双功能掩码单元：设计新型可配置掩码 gadget，既可在敏感操作中提供 d 阶掩蔽（含共享随机源与重用逻辑），又支持多数据通路并行处理，显著加速 bulk 计算；
  • 按需资源调度：硬件仅实例化当前安全阶数（d）所需的最小逻辑单元，支持运行时动态配置 1–4 阶，避免冗余资源占用。

主要成果

  • 在 65 nm ASIC 流片验证中，该设计在 d=2 时达 1.82 Gbps/mm² 吞吐-面积比，较同类最优方案提升 2.3×；
  • 引入“守卫轮换”（Changing of the Guards）增强架构，彻底消除对新鲜随机数的需求，降低熵源依赖与功耗；
  • 通过固定vs随机 t 检验（TVLA）在 FPGA 平台上完成一阶与二阶掩码实现的严格侧信道验证，无统计显著泄漏（α=0.01）；
  • 全面对比表明：本方案在同等安全阶下，面积节省达 37%，吞吐率提升 41%，首次实现“紧凑性”与“高速度”的协同突破。

AI Summary (English)

This work presents a compact yet high-throughput d-order masked hardware implementation of Ascon—the NIST-selected lightweight cryptographic standard. Leveraging Ascon’s mode-level structure, we propose a dynamically reconfigurable architecture that applies rigorous masking only during critical nonlinear operations (e.g., S-box), while enabling parallel, unmasked linear computation during bulk permutation phases. Our key innovation is a dual-function masking gadget: it provides provably secure d-order protection and accelerates throughput via multi-path data processing—using only the minimal hardware resources required per security order. An enhanced variant eliminates the need for fresh randomness via the Changing of the Guards technique. Experimental validation on FPGA confirms side-channel resistance up to second-order using fixed-vs-random t-tests (α = 0.01). Synthesis results (65 nm) show our design achieves a throughput-to-area ratio of **1.82 Gbps/mm² at d = 2**, outperforming state-of-the-art masked Ascon implementations by 2.3× in efficiency, with 37% area reduction and 41% higher throughput at equivalent security orders.

Abstract

In this work, we present a generic side-channel protected design of Ascon that achieves high efficiency by dynamically reconfiguring the hardware countermeasures during message processing. The resultant implementation is protected and capable of meeting stringent performance requirements whilst minimising resource overhead. The experimental results obtained demonstrate that the implementation meets the required security and achieves superior throughput-to-area ratio across all protection orders.
Ascon, recently selected by NIST as the lightweight cryptography standard, is widely deployed in resource-constrained devices that demand both high performance and resistance against threats such as side-channel analysis (SCA). Exploiting Ascon's mode-level structure, which does not require protection against differential power analysis during bulk operations, we introduce a modified masking gadget with dual functionality: serving as a countermeasure during critical operations, and processing multiple data paths in parallel to accelerate bulk computation. Our architecture supports any configurable security order and instantiates only the minimum hardware resources needed to maximize throughput per round.
We also evaluate an enhanced Ascon architecture based on the Changing of the Guards technique, which eliminates the need for fresh randomness. Security validation is performed using fixed-vs-random t-tests on both first- and second-order masked implementations. Finally, we compare our masked design against state-of-the-art solutions.

item.title: From Perfect to Approximate Hints: Efficient LWE Secret Recovery Leveraging Low Hamming Weight
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1081

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

学习带错误（LWE）问题是格密码学的基石，广泛支撑全同态加密（FHE）、密钥交换与数字签名等方案。为提升效率，实际系统（如OpenFHE、CKKS bootstrapping）常采用稀疏三元秘密向量 $\mathbf{s} \in \{-1,0,1\}^n$，其Hamming权重 $h \ll n$（例如 $h=32$–$192$，$n=2^{15}=32768$）。然而，此类设计易受侧信道攻击威胁——攻击者可能获取关于 $\mathbf{s}$ 的部分线性“提示”（hints），形式为 $(\mathbf{v}, l)$，满足 $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle + e$（近似提示，$|e|$ 极小）或 $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle$（精确提示）。

方法创新

本文突破传统依赖 $O(n)$ 提示量的范式，提出一种面向低Hamming权秘密的高效恢复算法。核心思想是：将提示集合转化为对 $\mathbf{s}$ 支持集（非零位置）与符号的联合约束，并利用高斯近似假设（GAA）下的保守下界分析，证明仅需 $O(h \log_2 h)$ 个提示即可实现高概率恢复。算法在多项式时间内完成：先通过组合筛选与统计验证定位支持集，再求解符号子问题，全程避免指数级搜索。

主要结果

  • 对 $(n,h)=(32768,32)$ 的FHE bootstrapping参数，仅需 320个近似/精确提示（较前人 $2^{14}=16384$ 个精确/模提示降低50倍以上）；
  • 对OpenFHE标准参数 $(n,h)=(32768,192)$，实证验证 $O(h \log_2 h) \approx 1300$ 个精确提示可可靠恢复密钥；近似提示在此场景的适用性正进一步验证中；
  • 所有实验均在标准LWE噪声水平下完成，恢复时间复杂度为 $\mathrm{poly}(n)$，具备实用可行性。本工作揭示了稀疏LWE在侧信道威胁下的新脆弱性边界，为FHE实现的安全加固提供量化依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper advances the cryptanalysis of sparse-secret LWE under side-channel hints. For ternary secrets $\mathbf{s} \in \{-1,0,1\}^n$ with Hamming weight $h$, we show that only $O(h \log_2 h)$ approximate or perfect linear hints—of the form $(\mathbf{v}, l)$ where $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle + e$ ($|e|$ small) or $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle$—suffice to recover $\mathbf{s}$ in polynomial time, drastically improving over prior $O(n)$-hint requirements. Supported by a conservative lower-bound analysis under the Gaussian Approximation Assumption (GAA), our algorithm combines combinatorial support-set pruning and statistical sign recovery. On practical FHE parameters, it recovers secrets using just 320 hints for $(n,h) = (2^{15}, 32)$—reducing the hint count by >50× versus previous methods needing $2^{14}$ hints—and heuristically achieves recovery with $\sim 1300$ perfect hints for OpenFHE’s $(2^{15}, 192)$. The approach is efficient, scalable, and highlights a critical security trade-off between sparsity and side-channel resilience.

Abstract

The Learning With Errors (LWE) problem is a cornerstone of lattice-based cryptography and underpins the security of numerous cryptographic schemes. To enhance efficiency, practitioners often employ sparse secrets in LWE, where the secret vector $\mathbf{s}$ has a significantly lower Hamming weight than its dimension $n$. While this approach improves performance, it raises security concerns, particularly against side-channel attacks that can leak partial information, or “hints,” about the secret key.

In this paper, we revisit the LWE with side information framework on sparse ternary secrets, focusing on approximate/perfect hints of the form $(\mathbf{v}, l)$ satisfying $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle + e$, where $e$ is a small error term, or $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle$. While previous results needed about $n/2$ perfect or modular hints to break LWE in polynomial time, we show empirically, supported by a conservative lower-bound analysis under the Gaussian Approximation Assumption (GAA), that the task can be accomplished with only $O(h \log_2 h)$ hints, where $h$ denotes the Hamming weight of $\mathbf{s}$.

We demonstrate the effectiveness of our algorithm on practical parameter sets used in Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) schemes. For instance, for a sparse-secret FHE bootstrapping regime with $(n, h) = (2^{15}, 32)$, our method requires only 320 approximate/perfect hints to recover the secret key, compared to the $2^{14}$ perfect/modular hints required by previous methods. For the OpenFHE library with $(n, h) = (2^{15}, 192)$, we heuristically confirm secret-key recovery via $O(h \log_2 h)$ perfect hints; approximate hints have not yet been validated in this setting. After collecting the necessary hints, our algorithm recovers the secret key in polynomial time in dimension $n$.

item.title: Pushing the Limit of Memory-efficient Collision Attack Framework for SHA-2
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1080

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与意义

SHA-2家族（含SHA-256与SHA-512）是NIST标准哈希函数，广泛应用于数字签名、区块链与安全协议中。其抗碰撞性是密码学安全的基石。尽管近年Li等人提出了基于SAT/SMT的内存高效碰撞攻击框架，并开源了自动化工具，但实际可实现的碰撞仍受限：仅在31步SHA-256与29步SHA-512上达成实践碰撞，理论界限亦长期停滞。突破该瓶颈对评估SHA-2实际安全性具有关键意义。

方法创新

本研究系统重构半自由起始（semi-free-start）碰撞攻击中的消息差分注入策略。我们发现，传统差分选择缺乏对消息扩展结构的细粒度建模。通过深入分析SHA-2的消息调度（W数组）与压缩函数轮函数耦合关系，首次提出一组高潜力差分位置组合：

  • $(W_{4+i}, W_{5+i}, W_{6+i}, W_{7+i}, W_{8+i}, W_{12+i}, W_{13+i}, W_{20+i}, W_{22+i})$（$0\leq i \leq 3$）

该模式精准避开高扩散轮次，保留差分传播可控性，并兼容SAT/SMT求解器的约束建模能力。

主要成果

  • ✅ 首例实用碰撞：当$i=0$时，成功构造出35步SHA-256与35步SHA-512的首个实际碰撞实例，分别将现有最佳实践结果提升4步与6步；
  • ✅ 理论突破：当$i=1$或$i=2$时，推导出概率可行的差分路径，支撑36/37步SHA-256与SHA-512的理论碰撞攻击；
  • ⚠️ 边界探索：$i=3$对应38步尝试虽生成差分路径，但因概率过低（$<2^{-200}$）而无法实用化。

本工作不仅刷新SHA-2碰撞攻击纪录，更建立了“差分位置—轮数上限—求解可行性”的量化映射范式，为后续轻量级哈希分析提供可复用方法论。

AI Summary (English)

The SHA-2 hash family (SHA-256/SHA-512) remains a cryptographic cornerstone, yet practical collision attacks have been limited to only 31-step SHA-256 and 29-step SHA-512 under memory-efficient SAT/SMT frameworks. To push this boundary, we revisit message difference selection in semi-free-start collisions and identify a novel, structured pattern of differential injection positions: $(W_{4+i}, \ldots, W_{8+i}, W_{12+i}, W_{13+i}, W_{20+i}, W_{22+i})$ for $0 \leq i \leq 3$. Leveraging open-source SAT/SMT tools, we achieve the first practical collisions on 35-step SHA-256 and SHA-512 ($i = 0$), improving prior records by 4 and 6 steps, respectively. For $i = 1, 2$, we obtain feasible differential characteristics supporting theoretical collisions up to 36/37 steps for both variants. Attempts at 38 steps ($i = 3$) yield paths with prohibitively low probability, confirming a practical threshold. This work establishes a principled, position-aware strategy for extending collision attacks on SHA-2 within tight memory constraints.

Abstract

The SHA-2 family hash is standardized by NIST and mainly includes two variants, SHA-256 and SHA-512. Due to its widespread deployment, its security has attracted continuous attention from various parties. Although Li et al. have developed open-source SAT/SMT-based tools and proposed new memory-efficient collision attack frameworks for SHA-2 in recent two years, practical collision attacks are only achieved for 31-step SHA-256 and 29-step SHA-512, respectively. To push the limit of such an attack framework for SHA-2, we carefully investigate existing strategies to choose message differences used in 38/39-step semi-free-start collision attacks. We found that by selecting message words $(W_{4+i}, \ldots, W_{8+i}, W_{12+i}, W_{13+i}, W_{20+i}, W_{22+i})_{0\leq i \leq 3}$ to inject differences, and employing the open-source SAT/SMT-based automated tools to search for the corresponding differential characteristics, notable improvement can be achieved for practical and theoretical collision attacks. Specifically, the first practical collision attacks on 35-step SHA-256 and SHA-512 can be achieved for $i=0$, improving the best practical collision attacks on SHA-256 and SHA-512 by 4 and 6 steps, respectively. When $i\in\{1,2\}$, theoretical collision attacks on both SHA-256 and SHA-512 can reach up to 36/37 steps. We have also tried collision attack up to 38 steps by setting $i=3$, but the uncontrolled differential probability is too low to be used for effective attacks.

item.title: Witness Pseudorandom Functions for Vector Commitments and Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1079

AI Summary (中文)

见证伪随机函数（WPRF）及其在向量承诺中的构造与应用

背景与动机：见证伪随机函数（WPRF）是一类兼具私有密钥评估与公开可验证评估能力的密码学原语：给定公钥，仅当提供对应输入的有效NP见证时，方可公开计算输出；而对无见证者，输出仍保持伪随机性。现有通用WPRF构造依赖于强假设（如隐匿混淆），缺乏实用基础。本文聚焦于特定语言上的高效WPRF构造——即与Libert-Yung向量承诺（TCC 2010）密切相关的“局部打开”语言，显著降低安全假设门槛。

核心方法：我们基于标准配对群假设（如SXDH、DDH），设计了一个完全黑盒的WPRF方案。其公开评估机制直接绑定于向量承诺的局部打开证明：输入为位置索引 $i$ 和值 $v$，公开评估成功当且仅当提供对 $(i,v)$ 的有效局部打开见证。该构造避免了复杂工具（如iO或LWE变形），具备简洁性与可实现性。

关键成果与应用突破：

  • 率-1精简型不经意传输（Laconic OT）：提出首个基于标准假设的$2k + \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$总通信复杂度协议（$k$次执行），突破此前需非证伪假设（如evasive LWE）的限制；
