  • 近最优精简型私有集合交集（PSI）：实现每发送方元素仅$\lambda$比特摊销通信（$\lambda$为元素位长），且通信量与接收方集合大小无关，逼近信息论下界；
  • 率-1批处理注册式加密（RBE）：支持多消息-多接收方加密，密文开销恒为仅两个群元素，首次达成理论最优率。

本工作首次将WPRF从理论构造落地为支撑多项通信效率瓶颈问题的实用基石，统一提升了安全计算的简洁性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)

We construct the first witness pseudorandom function (WPRF) for the language of valid local openings in the Libert-Yung vector commitment scheme, based solely on standard pairing-group assumptions (e.g., SXDH) and using fully black-box techniques—bypassing heavy tools like indistinguishability obfuscation. Public evaluation of our WPRF is possible iff a valid NP witness (i.e., a local opening proof) is supplied, while outputs remain pseudorandom without it. Leveraging this primitive, we resolve three long-standing open problems in communication-efficient secure computation: (1) Rate-1 laconic oblivious transfer with total communication $2k + \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$ for $k$ executions—achieving this under falsifiable assumptions for the first time; (2) Near-optimal laconic private set intersection with amortized $\lambda$ bits per sender element (independent of receiver set size), matching the information-theoretic lower bound up to a constant factor; and (3) Rate-1 batch registration-based encryption where ciphertext overhead is only two group elements, even for multiple messages to multiple receivers.

Abstract

A witness pseudorandom function (WPRF) is a PRF which has an additional mode of public evaluation. Given the public key, it can be evaluated publicly if one provides a valid NP witness for that input, while the output remains pseudorandom to anyone without such a witness. WPRFs are powerful objects, and general-purpose constructions are currently only known from assumptions that imply indistinguishability obfuscation.

In this work, we construct a WPRF for a specific language related to the Libert-Yung vector commitment (TCC 2010). More specifically, public evaluation of the WPRF on an input is possible if a valid local opening for that input is provided. Our construction relies only on standard assumptions on pairing groups and is fully black-box.

We further show that this primitive enables us to solve several open problems in the study of communication-efficient secure computation:

  • Rate-1 Laconic Oblivious Transfer. We construct a laconic oblivious transfer protocol with total communication complexity $2k+\mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$ for $k$ executions. Previously, achieving this efficiency required non-falsifiable assumptions such as evasive LWE (Wee, CRYPTO'24).
  • Near-optimal Laconic Private Set Intersection. We construct laconic private set intersection for which the amortized communication complexity approaches $\lambda$ bits per element in the sender's set (assuming each set element is represented by $\lambda$ bits) and is independent of the receiver's set. This is within a constant factor of the information-theoretic lower bound.
  • Rate-1 Batch Registration-Based Encryption. We construct a registration-based encryption scheme in which, when the encryptor sends multiple messages to multiple receivers, the ciphertext overhead is only two group elements.

item.title: Post-Quantum HAWK Signature Acceleration with RISC-V-Based Hardware-Software Co-Design
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1078

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

随着量子计算技术的快速发展，传统公钥密码面临严峻威胁，推动后量子密码（PQC）标准化进程加速。HAWK 是一种新兴的格基数字签名方案，也是NIST后量子密码标准化“附加数字签名”阶段第三轮中唯一入选的格密码候选者。相比NIST标准ML-DSA（Dilithium），HAWK具备更紧凑的密钥与签名尺寸；相比FN-DSA（FALCON），其设计简洁、无需浮点运算，显著降低硬件实现复杂度，特别适用于嵌入式、IoT等资源受限场景。

方法与创新

本文面向轻量级RISC-V平台开展软硬协同优化：在Vex RISC-V开源处理器核上进行HAWK签名/验证全流程软件性能剖析，精准识别Keccak置换与多项式数论变换（NTT/INTT） 为两大性能瓶颈。据此提出定制化硬件加速方案——通过RISC-V自定义功能单元（Custom Function Unit, CFU）集成专用加速器，并配套设计轻量级自定义指令（如keccak256, ntt8, intt8），实现关键密码原语的低开销卸载。

主要成果

我们构建了多个协同设计变体，在Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA上实现并评估：最优方案在保持极小面积开销前提下，实现签名生成≈3×加速（vs. 纯软件基线），验证延迟同步提升；整体面积-时间积（ATP）降低约40%，能效比显著优于现有FPGA实现。本工作首次为HAWK提供可综合、可复用的RISC-V协同加速框架，为后量子密码在边缘设备的实用化部署提供了高效可行的技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

The emergence of quantum computing necessitates efficient post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) implementations for resource-constrained devices. HAWK—a compact, lattice-based digital signature scheme and the sole NIST PQC Standardization Round 3 candidate in the “Additional Digital Signatures” category—offers advantages over ML-DSA (smaller keys/signatures) and FALCON (no floating-point arithmetic). We profiled HAWK’s software execution on a lightweight Vex RISC-V core and identified Keccak permutations and polynomial NTT/INTT operations as dominant bottlenecks. To address this, we propose a hardware-software co-design: integrating dedicated accelerators as RISC-V Custom Function Units (CFUs) with tailored instructions (e.g., keccak256, ntt8). Implemented on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA, our best variant achieves ≈3× speedup in signature generation and verification over the pure-software baseline, while reducing the area-time product by ≈40%. This work delivers the first practical, synthesizable RISC-V co-design for HAWK, enabling efficient PQC deployment on edge platforms.

Abstract

Advances in quantum computing technology have motivated the development of post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms. HAWK is a new post-quantum digital signature scheme and the only lattice-based candidate selected for Round 3 of the "Additional Digital Signatures" phase of the NIST PQC Standardization process. HAWK offers compact key and signature sizes compared to NIST standard ML-DSA (Dilithium), and its simple design avoids the use of floating-point arithmetic unlike NIST standard FN-DSA (FALCON). This makes HAWK very well suited for resource-constrained applications. We perform software runtime profiling of HAWK signature computation and verification on a resource-efficient Vex RISC-V processor core, and identify Keccak permutations and polynomial transformations as the most computationally expensive functions. In this work, we demonstrate light-weight hardware-software co-design of HAWK with these operations accelerated using Vex RISC-V Custom Function Units and accompanying custom instructions. We present multiple design variants with different degrees of acceleration, and our best design achieves $\approx 3 \times$ speedup and $\approx 40\%$ reduction in area-time-product compared to the baseline when implemented on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA.

item.title: Authenticated and Incremental Single-Server Private Information Retrieval
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1077

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

Authenticated Private Information Retrieval（认证型私有信息检索，AuthPIR）在单服务器模型下兼顾查询隐私性与服务器行为可验证性：客户端可无条件隐藏查询索引，同时在服务器篡改数据或伪造响应时安全中止。然而，现有单服务器AuthPIR方案面临两大瓶颈：（1）具备亚线性在线通信开销（如 $O(\sqrt{N})$）的构造缺乏完整、可复现的实现与实证分析；（2）支持数据库更新的“增量式”机制引入冗余计算——尤其在频繁小规模修改场景下，离线预处理开销激增。

方法与创新

本文首次实现了USENIX ’23提出的理论框架，并提出三个完全实例化、带严格安全性证明的方案：

  • LWE-AuthPIR 与 DDH-AuthPIR：均实现 $O(\sqrt{N})$ 在线通信，支持标准静态数据库检索，提供基于LWE假设或DDH假设的适应性安全证明；
  • LWE-AuthIncPIR：首创“单服务器认证且增量式PIR”（Authenticated and Incremental PIR）范式，支持即时单条目更新（无需全量重计算），并设计通信高效的行聚合协议（row-aggregation），专为周期性批量更新优化。

关键结果

在1GB数据库（约1亿条记录）上实验表明：当1%–8%条目更新时，LWE-AuthIncPIR相较前序增量方案降低离线预处理计算量19–88倍；在真实场景——密码泄露检测（如Have I Been Pwned）中，其预处理时间减少67倍，端到端通信开销降低2.9倍，显著提升实用性与部署可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Authenticated Private Information Retrieval (AuthPIR) enables private database queries with malicious-server detection, yet practical single-server schemes with sublinear online communication remain unimplemented, and existing incremental updates incur excessive overhead. This paper bridges the gap by presenting two fully concrete, provably secure AuthPIR schemes—LWE-AuthPIR and DDH-AuthPIR—both achieving $O(\sqrt{N})$ online communication. We further introduce the new primitive of single-server authenticated and incremental PIR and propose LWE-AuthIncPIR, supporting immediate per-entry updates and an efficient row-aggregation mechanism for periodic updates. Evaluations on a 1GB database show LWE-AuthIncPIR reduces offline preprocessing by 19–88× when 1%–8% entries are modified, and in password breach detection, it achieves 67× faster preprocessing and 2.9× lower communication overhead versus prior incremental approaches.

Abstract

Authenticated Private Information Retrieval (Authenticated PIR) allows the client to retrieve the desired database entry without revealing any information about the query, while safely aborting if malicious behavior by the server is detected (presented in USENIX '23). However, two key challenges remain: existing single-server authenticated PIR schemes with sublinear online communication have not yet been clearly and fully implemented; incremental updates to the digest introduce unnecessary overhead. In this paper, we implement the previously outlined idea and present two complete and concrete single-server authenticated PIR schemes with $O(\sqrt{N})$ online communication, namely LWE-AuthPIR and DDH-AuthPIR, along with detailed security proofs. Furthermore, we introduce the notion of single-server authenticated and incremental PIR and propose a corresponding concrete construction, LWE-AuthIncPIR. LWE-AuthIncPIR supports immediate updates to individual entries and integrates a communication-efficient row aggregation for periodic update scenarios. When 1\%-8\% of the entries in a 1GB database are modified, LWE-AuthIncPIR reduces offline preprocessing computation by 19-88$\times$ compared to previous incremental update method. In the password breach detection, LWE-AuthIncPIR achieves a 67$\times$ reduction in preprocessing time and a 2.9$\times$ reduction in communication overhead.

item.title: Decentralizing Traitor Tracing: A Multi-Authority Approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1076

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统叛徒追踪（Traitor Tracing）（Chor-Fiat-Naor, CRYPTO’94）依赖单一可信权威生成主密钥，导致固有密钥托管（key escrow）风险——该权威可解密所有密文。这一中心化设计与端到端加密广播网络（如安全消息群组、DRM流媒体）的根本需求相冲突，严重制约其现实部署。

创新模型：多权威叛徒追踪（MA-TT）

本文正式提出并构建 多权威叛徒追踪（Multi-Authority Traitor Tracing, MA-TT） 框架（基于Goyal-Yadugiri的初步构想）。在MA-TT中，系统初始化由 $K$ 个异步、非交互、彼此不信任的权威共同完成；每位用户需从每个权威获取一个部分密钥才能解密，单点失效或合谋均无法恢复完整能力。

安全保障

  • 语义安全性：即使任意子集权威被攻破，只要对每个用户至少存在一个诚实权威未泄露其对应部分密钥，密文仍保持不可区分；
  • 强追踪性：任何被成功追踪指控的用户，必已完全泄露全部 $K$ 个部分密钥；若任一 honest 部分密钥仍被隐藏，则该用户绝不会被误判为叛徒。

构造方法与实现

我们提出新原语——分布式混合函数加密（Distributed Mixed Functional Encryption, DMFE），作为MA-TT的核心枢纽，并将其与任意多权威属性加密（MA-ABE）方案组合。DMFE基于LWE假设，利用单密钥密钥同态私有约束PRF实现；结合现有MA-ABE，获得：

  • LWE + 双线性对方案：密文大小为 $K \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda, \log N)$；
  • 通用构造：仅需任意公钥加密（PKE）或任意多权威函数加密（MA-FE），分别支持 $K \cdot N \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$ 密文与完全简洁参数。

本工作首次实现了去中心化、抗合谋、无密钥托管的叛徒追踪，为隐私优先的规模化加密广播提供了理论基石与实用路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Multi-Authority Traitor Tracing (MA-TT), a decentralized model that eliminates the key-escrow problem inherent in classical traitor tracing. In MA-TT, setup is distributed across $K$ asynchronous, non-interactive authorities; a user decrypts only by combining one partial key from each authority. Security requires: (1) semantic security even if an arbitrary subset of authorities is corrupted—provided at least one honest partial key remains hidden per user; and (2) traceability that never falsely accuses any user for whom at least one honest partial key is still concealed. We construct MA-TT by composing any Multi-Authority ABE (MA-ABE) with a new primitive, Distributed Mixed Functional Encryption (DMFE)—a careful decentralization of mixed FE. Building DMFE from LWE via single-key key-homomorphic private constrained PRFs, we obtain MA-TT from LWE + pairings with ciphertext size $K \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda, \log N)$. Feasibility extensions yield MA-TT from any PKE (ciphertext $K \cdot N \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$) or any MA-FE (fully succinct). This is the first traitor tracing scheme achieving provable decentralization, collusion resilience, and zero key escrow.

Abstract

Traitor tracing [Chor-Fiat-Naor; CRYPTO'94] has historically been formalized through the lens of a $\textit{single}$ trusted authority that samples the master keys and that, therefore, can read every ciphertext on its own. This $\textit{key escrow}$ problem makes traditional traitor tracing fundamentally incompatible with end-to-end encrypted broadcast networks. In this work, we study introduce $\textit{multi-authority traitor tracing}$ (MA-TT) [Goyal-Yadugiri; ePrint] a new decentralized model for traitor tracing in which the setup is split across $K$ asynchronous and non-interacting authorities, and a user can decrypt only by combining partial keys from $\textit{every}$ authority. We require that semantic security holds even when an arbitrary set of authorities is corrupted (as long as some honest partial key remains hidden for every user), and that traceability never accuses a user for whom an honest partial key remains hidden.

We design MA-TT by combining any multi-authority attribute-based encryption (MA-ABE) scheme with a new primitive that we introduce, distributed mixed functional encryption (DMFE), a careful decentralization of the mixed functional encryption notion [Goyal-Koppula-Waters; STOC'18]. We construct DMFE from LWE via single-key key-homomorphic private constrained PRFs. Plugging in known MA-ABE schemes, we obtain MA-TT from LWE plus pairings with ciphertext size $K \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda, \log N)$. As feasibility, we also give MA-TT from any PKE (with ciphertexts of size $K \cdot N \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$) and from any multi-authority functional encryption (with fully succinct parameters).
